## Deep Security Review -- 2026-06-05 15:00 UTC

### F828: WebSocket token blacklist bypass -- logged-out tokens still accepted

- Area: Backend / SupportWebSocketHandler
- Environment: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/websocket/SupportWebSocketHandler.java:240-261`
- Severity: **CRITICAL**
- Description: `authenticate()` calls `jwtService.isTokenValid()` which checks expiration and password-change time only. It does NOT call `tokenBlacklistService.isAccessTokenBlacklisted(jti)`. After logout, the token is blacklisted for HTTP via `JwtAuthenticationFilter`, but WebSocket connections still accept it.
- Status: OPEN
- Suggested fix: Extract JTI from token and check blacklist before accepting WebSocket connection.

### F829: CSRF protection globally disabled with credentials-enabled CORS

- Area: Backend / SecurityConfig
- Environment: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/config/SecurityConfig.java:50,158`
- Severity: **HIGH**
- Description: `.csrf().disable()` with `setAllowCredentials(true)` in CORS. Browser clients with cookies are vulnerable to CSRF on state-changing `permitAll()` endpoints (payment callbacks, guest checkout, admin bootstrap).
- Status: OPEN
- Suggested fix: Implement `CookieCsrfTokenRepository` or document the token-only API decision. Ensure webhook signature verification on all callback endpoints.

### F830: Missing Content-Security-Policy and X-Frame-Options headers

- Area: Backend / SecurityConfig
- Environment: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/config/SecurityConfig.java:57-64`
- Severity: **HIGH**
- Description: Security headers set `X-Content-Type-Options`, `HSTS`, and `Referrer-Policy` but omit `Content-Security-Policy` and `X-Frame-Options`. Pages can be iframe-embedded (clickjacking) and have no browser-side XSS mitigation.
- Status: OPEN
- Suggested fix: Add `.frameOptions().deny()` and a restrictive CSP directive.

### F831: Unbounded pagination size in SearchController

- Area: Backend / SearchController
- Environment: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/SearchController.java:35-36`
- Severity: **MEDIUM**
- Description: `searchProducts()` accepts `size` parameter without upper-bound validation. Attacker can send `size=999999999` causing memory exhaustion.
- Status: OPEN
- Suggested fix: Add `Math.min(size, 100)` or `@Max(100)` annotation.

### F832: Unbounded limit parameters on product endpoints

- Area: Backend / ProductController
- Environment: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/ProductController.java:93,110-111,117-118,122`
- Severity: **MEDIUM**
- Description: `getFeaturedProducts()`, `getAddOnCandidates()`, `getFinderCandidates()` accept unbounded `limit`. `getProductsByIds()` accepts unbounded ID list. All are `permitAll()`.
- Status: OPEN
- Suggested fix: Cap `limit` at 100 and `ids` list at 50.

### F833: Guest checkout has no email verification

- Area: Backend / OrderController, OrderService
- Environment: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/OrderController.java:83-89`
- Severity: **MEDIUM**
- Description: `guestCheckout` accepts any `guestEmail` without verification. Attacker can place orders with victim's email, gaining access to guest support/tracking via email+orderNo.
- Status: OPEN
- Suggested fix: Require email OTP verification before completing guest checkout.

### F834: Missing @Valid on AdminController request bodies

- Area: Backend / AdminController
- Environment: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/AdminController.java:174,200,257,339,684`
- Severity: **MEDIUM**
- Description: `createProduct()`, `updateProduct()`, `createBrand()`, `createCategory()`, `broadcastNotification()` lack `@Valid` annotation. Validation constraints on entities are never enforced.
- Status: OPEN
- Suggested fix: Add `@Valid` to all `@RequestBody` parameters.

### F835: Admin bootstrap endpoint has no global account limit

- Area: Backend / UserController
- Environment: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/UserController.java:136-163`
- Severity: **MEDIUM**
- Description: `POST /users/create-admin` is `permitAll()` with rate limit (3/hour/IP) but no global cap on total admin accounts. Compromised bootstrap token + distributed IPs = unlimited admins.
- Status: CURRENT_SOURCE_COVERED
- Suggested fix: Already covered in current source by `UserService.registerAdmin()` acquiring `GET_LOCK('shop_admin_bootstrap', 10)` and rejecting when `countAdminUsers() > 0` before insert.

### F836: JWT uses symmetric HS256 algorithm

- Area: Backend / JwtService
- Environment: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/security/JwtService.java:54`
- Severity: **LOW**
- Description: HS256 means same secret for signing and verification. In microservice architecture (shop-gateway), every service needs the shared secret, increasing blast radius.
- Status: OPEN
- Suggested fix: Migrate to RS256 (asymmetric) for token signing.

### F837: Cart update quantity race condition window

- Area: Backend / CartService
- Environment: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/CartService.java:84-107`
- Severity: **LOW**
- Description: `updateQuantity()` reads cart item without lock at line 86, then acquires `FOR UPDATE` lock at line 91. Between these, another transaction can modify the item. The productId check at line 94 is not a reliable safety check.
- Status: OPEN
- Suggested fix: Re-read cart item after acquiring lock and compare with initial snapshot.

### F838: WebSocket handler does not rate-limit individual messages

- Area: Backend / SupportWebSocketHandler
- Environment: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/websocket/SupportWebSocketHandler.java:104-183`
- Severity: **LOW**
- Description: Connection limits exist but no per-session message rate limiting. A connected user can flood the support system with messages.
- Status: OPEN
- Suggested fix: Add per-session sliding window rate limiter (max N messages/minute).

## Deep Frontend UX Review -- 2026-06-05 15:00 UTC

### F839: Module-level mutable state leaks between user sessions

- Area: Frontend / ProductDetail, ProductList, Cart
- Environment: `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.tsx:49-50`, `ProductList.tsx:201-203`, `Cart.tsx:36`
- Severity: **CRITICAL**
- Description: Several pages maintain module-level `Map`/`Set`/`let` variables that persist across user sessions. If User A logs out and User B logs in, stale cached data bleeds into User B's session. `Cart.tsx` `recentProductsCache` is never cleared on session change.
- Status: OPEN
- Suggested fix: Clear all module-level caches in auth session change handler, or scope caches inside component state.

### F840: ProductDetail uses `any`-typed product state

- Area: Frontend / ProductDetail
- Environment: `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.tsx:159,170,77`
- Severity: **HIGH**
- Description: `useState<any>(null)` for core product state and `useState<any[]>([])` for recommendations. TypeScript type safety completely defeated for 2000+ line component.
- Status: OPEN
- Suggested fix: Type as `ProductPublic | null` and `ProductPublic[]`.

### F841: WebSocket reconnect loops have no maximum retry cap

- Area: Frontend / SupportManagement, CustomerSupportWidget
- Environment: `frontend/src/pages/SupportManagement.tsx:362-438`, `frontend/src/components/CustomerSupportWidget.tsx:418`
- Severity: **HIGH**
- Description: Both WebSocket reconnect loops increase delay via `getReconnectDelayMs` but never stop. If server is permanently down, infinite reconnect attempts continue.
- Status: OPEN
- Suggested fix: Add max retry count (e.g., 10) then show connection failure message.

### F842: Payment status polling never stops on permanent failure

- Area: Frontend / Checkout, Profile
- Environment: `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.tsx:1122-1155`, `Profile.tsx:580-597`
- Severity: **MEDIUM**
- Description: `setInterval` polling every 5 seconds for payment status has no max duration. If payment gateway is down, polling continues forever.
- Status: OPEN
- Suggested fix: Add 10-minute timeout, then show "payment verification timed out" with manual retry.

### F843: Module-level API caches not fully cleared on session change

- Area: Frontend / API layer
- Environment: `frontend/src/api/index.ts:549-608`
- Severity: **HIGH**
- Description: 25+ module-level `Map` caches persist for application lifetime. `clearUserScopedCaches` only clears user-scoped ones; product/order caches retain stale entries from previous sessions.
- Status: OPEN
- Suggested fix: Clear all caches on auth session change, not just user-scoped ones.

### F844: cachedGet bypasses deduplication when AbortSignal is provided

- Area: Frontend / API layer
- Environment: `frontend/src/api/index.ts:634-657`
- Severity: **MEDIUM**
- Description: When `options?.signal` is truthy, `cachedGet` calls `loader()` directly without checking pending requests. Concurrent requests with signals are not deduplicated.
- Status: OPEN
- Suggested fix: Track in-flight requests even when signal is provided.

### F845: Transient retry sleep does not respect AbortSignal

- Area: Frontend / API layer
- Environment: `frontend/src/api/index.ts:458,1356`
- Severity: **MEDIUM**
- Description: `sleep()` uses `window.setTimeout` without AbortSignal awareness. If user navigates away during retry delay, sleep continues for up to 900ms.
- Status: OPEN
- Suggested fix: Make `sleep` abort-aware by accepting an AbortSignal.

### F846: NotificationManagement renders HTML via dangerouslySetInnerHTML

- Area: Frontend / NotificationManagement
- Environment: `frontend/src/pages/NotificationManagement.tsx:233`
- Severity: **HIGH**
- Description: `dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: safePreviewHtml }}` depends entirely on `sanitizeHtml` correctness. If sanitization has bypass, this is XSS vector.
- Status: OPEN
- Suggested fix: Audit `sanitizeHtml.ts` for bypass vectors; use allowlist-only approach.

### F847: Multiple components missing disposed/AbortController guards

- Area: Frontend / Home, Cart, CouponManagement
- Environment: `frontend/src/pages/Home.tsx:460-499`, `Cart.tsx:87-114`, `CouponManagement.tsx:240`
- Severity: **MEDIUM**
- Description: `Home.fetchHome` fires `Promise.all` without AbortController. `Cart.fetchCartItems` has no disposed flag. `CouponManagement` timer can fire on unmounted component. All cause React state-update-on-unmounted-component warnings and potential memory leaks.
- Status: OPEN
- Suggested fix: Add disposed flags or AbortController to all async operations.

### F848: Accessibility -- thumbnail images missing keyboard handlers

- Area: Frontend / ProductDetail
- Environment: `frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.tsx:1208-1219`
- Severity: **MEDIUM**
- Description: Thumbnail images have `role="button"` and `tabIndex={0}` but no `onKeyDown` handler. Keyboard users can focus but cannot activate with Enter/Space.
- Status: OPEN
- Suggested fix: Add `onKeyDown` handler for Enter/Space, or wrap in `<button>`.

### F849: 224 uses of `any` type in page components

- Area: Frontend / Multiple pages
- Severity: **MEDIUM**
- Description: Page components contain 224 instances of `any` type, undermining TypeScript safety. Most critical: `ProductDetail.tsx:159` (product state), `SecurityAuditLogManagement.tsx:1050` (date range).

### F850: useAuth login error discards server message

## Deep Performance Review -- 2026-06-05 15:00 UTC

### F851: Unbounded findAll() loads entire product catalog into memory

- Area: Backend / ProductServiceImpl
- Environment: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/impl/ProductServiceImpl.java`
- Severity: **HIGH**
- Description: Multiple methods previously called no-arg `productRepository.findAll()` loading all products including heavy fields. Current source caps legacy reads and delegates public/discount paths to paged queries.
- Status: FIXED / SOURCE_FIXED (covered by TEST F9304)
- Fixed: 2026-06-08 08:51 UTC
- Fix evidence: `ProductSearchServiceTest` covers bounded legacy all/public/discount list calls and verifies no no-arg repository `findAll()` is used; broader targeted Maven passed.
- Suggested fix: Use paginated queries with column projection. Push filters into SQL WHERE clauses.

### F852: Order endpoints return all orders without pagination

- Area: Backend / OrderService, OrderController, OrderMapper
- Environment: `OrderService.java:919-923`, `OrderController.java:112-129`, `OrderMapper.xml:381-384`
- Severity: **HIGH**
- Description: `GET /orders/me` and `GET /orders/user/{userId}` return ALL orders. SQL has no LIMIT clause. Power users with thousands of orders cause memory + network exhaustion.
- Status: FIXED / CURRENT_SOURCE_COVERED / REGRESSION_GUARD_CONFIRMED
- Suggested fix: Add pagination parameters to controller, service, and SQL.
- Current-source evidence: `/orders/me` and customer-compatible user order reads now route through bounded page/size parameters, `OrderService.getOrdersByUserId(userId, page, size)`, `OrderRepository.findByUserIdPage(...)`, and mapper `LIMIT #{limit} OFFSET #{offset}`. `OrderStatsServiceTest` rejects reintroducing an unbounded customer order mapper path, and `OrderControllerCustomerPaginationTest` covers controller pagination behavior.

### F853: N+1 query in OrderService.restoreStock()

- Area: Backend / OrderService
- Environment: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/OrderService.java:1299-1312`
- Severity: **HIGH**
- Description: `restoreStock()` calls `findById` and `save` per order item in a loop. Cancelling an order with N items generates 2N database queries.
- Status: FIXED / SOURCE_FIXED / REGRESSION_GUARD_ADDED
- Suggested fix: Batch-load with `findAllById`, batch-save with `saveAll`.
- Fix evidence: `cancelPendingPaymentOrder`, `finalizeCompletedReturnRefund`, and `refundOrder` now pass the whole item list to grouped restoration. Simple rows use one atomic stock increment per product, while variant rows use one lock/save per product because variant stock is stored in product JSON. Regression coverage verifies grouped simple and variant behavior and rejects the old per-item loop pattern.

### F854: N+1 query in OrderService.shipOrder() loads all carriers

- Environment: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/OrderService.java:1330-1333`
- Severity: **MEDIUM**

- Status: FIXED / DUPLICATE_COVERED_BY_F3476 (2026-06-09 00:39 UTC)
- Resolution: `OrderService.shipOrder(...)` now resolves the carrier through `LogisticsCarrierService.findByTrackingCode(...)`, backed by `LogisticsCarrierRepository.findByTrackingCodeIgnoreCase(...)`, and `PaymentFlowServiceTest` verifies `findAll(false)` is not called during shipment.

- Environment: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/impl/CategoryServiceImpl.java:34-35,157-225`

- Description: `findAll()` loads all categories then runs multiple `findByParentIdIn` at each depth level. Individual queries per node.
- Status: OPEN

### F862: Correlated subqueries in order summary aggregation

- Area: Backend / OrderMapper
- Environment: `src/main/resources/mapper/OrderMapper.xml:312-329`
- Severity: **MEDIUM**
- Description: `countAdminOrderSummary` uses correlated `EXISTS` subqueries inside `COUNT(CASE...)`. Executes per row.

- Area: Frontend / ProductList

- Description: Product cards rendered in grid without virtualization. Page size up to 100 cards all in DOM simultaneously.

### F864: Home page fires all API calls on mount without lazy loading

- Description: Featured, discount, and recommendation sections all fire API calls on initial mount. Below-fold sections should defer until scrolled near.

### F865: AdminLayout infinite spinner on unknown admin route

- Environment: `frontend/src/components/AdminLayout.tsx:196-202`

## Live API Testing -- 2026-05-29

### F866: Products API pagination completely broken — all products returned regardless of page/size

- Area: Backend / ProductController
- Environment: `GET /api/products?page=0&size=5` vs `GET /api/products`
- Steps: Call `GET /api/products?page=0&size=5`, `GET /api/products?page=1&size=5`, `GET /api/products?page=100&size=5`
- Expected: Each call returns a different subset of products based on page/size
- Actual: All calls return all 29 products regardless of page/size parameters. Pagination is completely non-functional.
- Status: OPEN
- Evidence: `curl -s "https://pet.686888666.xyz/api/products?page=0&size=5" | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(len(json.load(sys.stdin)))"` returns 29
- Severity: **CRITICAL** — storefront pagination UI shows products but all pages are identical
- Suggested fix: Implement `Pageable` parameter in Spring controller or add LIMIT/OFFSET to SQL query

### F867: Products API sorting completely ignored

- Area: Backend / ProductController
- Environment: `GET /api/products?sort=price`, `GET /api/products?sort=price,asc`, `GET /api/products?sort=name`
- Steps: Call products API with sort=price, sort=price,asc, sort=price,desc, sort=name
- Expected: Products returned in sorted order
- Actual: All sort parameters are ignored; products always returned in same default order (id=1 first)
- Status: OPEN
- Evidence: All sort variants return `1: 129.9, 2: 49.9, 3: 34.9, 4: 89.9, 5: 18.9` (no change)
- Severity: **HIGH** — sort functionality visible in UI but produces no effect
- Suggested fix: Implement `Sort` parameter handling in the controller/service layer

### F868: Featured products filter returns all products regardless of value

- Area: Backend / ProductController
- Environment: `GET /api/products?featured=true`, `GET /api/products?featured=false`
- Steps: Call with featured=true, featured=false
- Expected: featured=true returns only featured products; featured=false returns non-featured
- Actual: Both return all 29 products
- Status: OPEN
- Severity: **HIGH** — featured sections show all products instead of curated selection
- Suggested fix: Add `WHERE is_featured = true` condition when featured parameter is present

### F869: Search API requires authentication — blocks guest product discovery

- Area: Backend / SearchController
- Environment: `GET /api/search?q=cat` returns 401 without token
- Steps: Call `/api/search?q=cat` without Authorization header
- Expected: Search results returned (public endpoint for e-commerce)
- Actual: Returns 401 Unauthorized
- Status: OPEN
- Severity: **HIGH** — guest users cannot search products, major conversion funnel blocker
- Suggested fix: Mark `/api/search` as `permitAll()` in SecurityConfig

### F870: Cart API requires userId parameter instead of using JWT identity

- Area: Backend / CartController
- Environment: `GET /api/cart` with valid JWT returns `{error: "userId is required"}`
- Steps: Login, get JWT token, call `GET /api/cart` with Authorization header
- Expected: Cart contents for authenticated user returned
- Actual: Returns 400 "userId is required" — must pass userId as query param
- Status: OPEN
- Severity: **MEDIUM** — API design flaw; userId should be extracted from JWT token
- Suggested fix: Extract userId from `Authentication` principal instead of requiring query param

### F871: Orders listing requires admin permission — regular users cannot see their orders

- Area: Backend / OrderController
- Environment: `GET /api/orders` with valid user JWT returns 403
- Steps: Login as regular user, call `GET /api/orders`
- Expected: User's own orders returned
- Actual: Returns 403 "Admin permission required"
- Status: OPEN
- Severity: **HIGH** — users cannot view their order history from API
- Suggested fix: Add `/api/orders/me` endpoint for user's own orders, or change `/api/orders` to return user-scoped results

### F872: Rate limiting too aggressive — IP banned after ~15 failed attempts

- Area: Backend / Security
- Environment: Login endpoint rate limiting
- Steps: Attempt ~15 login requests with wrong password
- Expected: Gradual rate limiting with clear error messages
- Actual: IP returns 000 (connection refused) — completely blocked
- Status: OPEN
- Severity: **MEDIUM** — legitimate users with typos get permanently banned; no self-service unban
- Suggested fix: Implement progressive delays instead of hard bans; add captcha after N failures

### F873: Inconsistent API error response formats

- Area: Backend / Global error handling
- Environment: Multiple endpoints
- Steps: Trigger various errors (400, 401, 403, 404, 500)
- Expected: Consistent error response format across all endpoints
- Actual: Some return `{error: "message"}`, some `{error: "type", message: "detail", status: N}`, some bare strings
- Status: OPEN
- Severity: **LOW** — inconsistent API contract makes frontend error handling harder
- Suggested fix: Standardize error response format with `ErrorResponse` DTO

### F874: Empty categoryId parameter returns all products instead of empty set

- Area: Backend / ProductController
- Environment: `GET /api/products?categoryId=`
- Steps: Call with empty categoryId
- Expected: Either validation error or no products
- Actual: Returns all 29 products (ignores empty filter)
- Status: OPEN
- Severity: **LOW** — edge case; could confuse API consumers
- Suggested fix: Treat empty string as null and validate appropriately

### F875: Path traversal attempt returns 200 instead of 400

- Area: Backend / Input validation
- Environment: `GET /api/products?search=../../etc/passwd`
- Steps: Call with path traversal in search param
- Expected: 400 Bad Request or sanitized input
- Actual: Returns 200 (no products found, but request accepted)
- Status: OPEN
- Severity: **LOW** — not exploitable (no products match) but input not rejected
- Suggested fix: Add input sanitization to reject directory traversal patterns

### F552: Semantic Ant Design images lacked alternative text

- Area: Frontend / Admin catalog management and storefront stock alerts accessibility
- Environment: `frontend/src/pages/BrandManagement.tsx`, `frontend/src/pages/CategoryManagement.tsx`, `frontend/src/pages/ProductManagement.tsx`, and `frontend/src/pages/StockAlerts.tsx`
- Steps: Navigate with assistive technology through brand logo thumbnails/previews, category image thumbnails/previews, product admin thumbnails/previews, URL-import image previews, or stock-alert product images.
- Expected: Meaningful images should expose an `alt` value based on the represented brand, category, product, or localized preview label.
- Actual: Several Ant Design `Image` components rendered without `alt`, leaving semantic catalog and stock-alert images unnamed for assistive technology and possibly unhelpful when image loading fails.
- Status: FIXED

- Retest: 2026-06-01 23:48 UTC static source review confirmed all Ant Design `Image` usages under `frontend/src/pages` and `frontend/src/components` now include `alt`. Targeted source scans and `git diff --check` passed for touched files. No Maven/Jest/build/UI smoke/screenshot/commit was run per instruction.
- Non-issues recorded: Decorative placeholder divs remain without alt because they are not images. Existing raw brand/product/category names are used as record-specific catalog labels, while fallback preview labels use existing localized UI text.
- Resolution: Semantic catalog and stock-alert images now have meaningful alternative text across admin and storefront surfaces.

### F551: Several media and delete icon controls lacked localized accessible names

- Area: Frontend / Storefront and customer account accessibility
- Environment: `frontend/src/components/ProductRichDetail.tsx`, `frontend/src/pages/Cart.tsx`, and `frontend/src/pages/Notifications.tsx`
- Steps: Navigate with a screen reader or keyboard to a direct MP4 rich-detail video, a mobile cart item delete button, a saved-for-later delete button, or a notification delete button.
- Expected: Direct videos should expose the localized product-video label already supplied to rich-detail rendering, and icon-only delete buttons should expose a localized action name.
- Actual: Direct rich-detail videos rendered with controls but no accessible name. The mobile cart delete button, saved-for-later delete button, and notification delete button were icon-only controls without `aria-label` or `title`.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static source review at 2026-06-01 23:42 UTC found `<video ... controls ... />` without a label in `ProductRichDetail.tsx`, plus icon-only `DeleteOutlined` buttons in Cart and Notifications.
- Retest: 2026-06-01 23:42 UTC static source review confirmed direct videos now use `block.caption || labels.videoTitle(index + 1)` as their `aria-label`, and the affected delete icon buttons now use localized `common.delete` for `aria-label` and `title`. Targeted source scans and `git diff --check` passed for touched files. No Maven/Jest/build/UI smoke/screenshot/commit was run per instruction.
- Non-issues recorded: Popconfirm confirmation text was already localized, but it does not name the closed trigger button for assistive technology. Product-rich-detail iframe and external-link video paths already had caption/localized title or link text fallbacks.
- Resolution: Key direct video and delete icon controls now have localized accessible names across storefront cart, notifications, and rich product detail surfaces.

### F545: Invalid support WebSocket payloads showed a hard-coded English warning

- Area: Frontend / Customer and admin support chat WebSocket error handling i18n polish
- Environment: `frontend/src/utils/supportChatConfig.ts`, `frontend/src/components/CustomerSupportWidget.tsx`, and `frontend/src/pages/SupportManagement.tsx`
- Steps: In Chinese or Spanish UI, receive a malformed support WebSocket frame such as invalid JSON, a JSON string, or a JSON array instead of an object.
- Expected: The local parser's own invalid-payload fallback should use the existing localized support rejection copy, while preserving any explicit backend-provided error message.
- Actual: `parseSupportSocketPayload(...)` returned `{ type: 'ERROR', message: 'Invalid support message' }` for malformed or non-object frames. Both Customer Support Widget and Support Management display `payload.message || t('pages.support.messageRejected')`, so the parser fallback bypassed the localized copy and showed English text in non-English UI.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static source review at 2026-06-01 23:06 UTC found the hard-coded parser fallback feeding user-visible `message.warning(...)` calls.
- Retest: 2026-06-01 23:06 UTC static source review confirmed malformed local WebSocket payloads now return `{ type: 'ERROR' }`, allowing both support UIs to fall back to `pages.support.messageRejected`. The related utility expectation was updated, targeted old-string scans passed, and `git diff --check` passed for touched files. No Maven/Jest/build/UI smoke/screenshot/commit was run per instruction.
- Non-issues recorded: Explicit backend-sent support error messages still display through `payload.message` because those are server-provided operational messages. Normal support message/session payload handling and reconnection behavior remain unchanged.
- Resolution: Locally rejected support WebSocket frames no longer leak English warning text into localized customer/admin chat surfaces.

Targeted fallback-label scans and `git diff --check` passed for the touched file. No Maven/Jest/build/UI smoke/screenshot/commit was run per instruction.

### F543: Selected option specs kept standard option labels in English

- Area: Frontend / Storefront cart, checkout, order, support, and admin order selected-spec i18n polish
- Environment: `frontend/src/utils/selectedSpecs.ts`, `frontend/src/pages/Cart.tsx`, `frontend/src/components/CartDrawer.tsx`, `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.tsx`, `frontend/src/pages/OrderTracking.tsx`, `frontend/src/pages/Profile.tsx`, `frontend/src/components/CustomerSupportWidget.tsx`, and `frontend/src/pages/OrderManagement.tsx`
- Steps: Switch the UI to Chinese or Spanish, add a product with standard stored options such as `Size`, `Color`, `Small`, or `Orange`, then inspect selected-spec text in the cart table/mobile card, cart drawer, checkout summary, order tracking, profile order detail, customer support order detail, and admin order detail/export surfaces.
- Expected: Standard product option names and common option values should use the same localized labels already used by Product Detail and Product List selectors, while custom merchant option names/values remain raw.
- Actual: `formatSelectedSpecs(...)` rendered every stored selected spec as `${name}: ${option}`. Standard English option keys and values therefore remained visible as `Size: Small` or `Color: Orange` in non-English cart/order/support surfaces even though selector surfaces already localized them.
- Status: FIXED

- Retest: 2026-06-01 22:54 UTC static source review confirmed selected-spec display now localizes known option names through the product spec/option label helpers and localizes common option values when the current language is available. Cart, Cart Drawer, Checkout, Order Tracking, Profile, Customer Support Widget, and Order Management now pass the active language to `formatSelectedSpecs(...)`. Targeted selected-spec scans and `git diff --check` passed for touched files. No Maven/Jest/build/UI smoke/screenshot/commit was run per instruction.
- Non-issues recorded: Product Detail and Product List option selectors already used `getLocalizedOptionLabel(...)`. Product Management option group names, variant option JSON examples, SKU values, and unknown custom option names/values remain raw catalog/operator data and backend contract values. Existing no-language utility/test calls keep the raw fallback behavior.
- Resolution: Customers and operators now see localized standard selected options across cart, checkout, order, support, and admin order surfaces without changing stored selected-spec payloads or variant matching.

### F542: User Management custom role labels could ignore configured role names

- Area: Frontend / Admin User Management role display i18n and operator readability

- Actual: `formatRoleLabel(...)` translated `pages.adminUsers.roleValues.${role}` for every role and checked whether the translated label equaled the key. Targeted role-label scans and `git diff --check` passed for touched files. No Maven/Jest/build/UI smoke/screenshot/commit was run per instruction.
- Non-issues recorded: Raw role codes remain unchanged in permission checks, role assignment payloads, filters, effective-role resolution, and role color lookup because those are backend contract values. Built-in role labels remain localized through existing English, Chinese, and Spanish locale keys.
- Resolution: Admin operators now see configured custom role names instead of i18n-generated placeholder labels.

### F540: Mobile Web navbar controls lacked the final 44px touch-target guard

- Area: Frontend / Storefront mobile navigation touch targets
- Environment: `frontend/src/components/Navbar.css`, regular mobile Web navbar header actions, account/cart/more/admin icon controls, search input/search button, suggestion chips, and category rail chips
- Steps: Open the storefront on a narrow mobile Web viewport outside the native App shell and inspect the cascaded mobile navbar CSS for the header icon actions, auth/account links, search controls, suggestion chips, and category rail buttons.
- Expected: Frequently used mobile navigation controls should retain at least 44px hit areas after late density and narrow-viewport rules, matching the existing native App touch-target guard.
- Actual: Late regular mobile Web rules compressed header actions and auth links to 32-36px, search controls to 34-38px, and category/suggestion chips to 32-34px. The existing 44px closure only applied under `body.shop-mobile-app`, leaving normal mobile Web users with smaller tap targets.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static CSS cascade review at 2026-06-01 22:36 UTC found regular mobile Web navbar controls below the 44px target while equivalent native App selectors already had a final guard.
- Retest: 2026-06-01 22:36 UTC static source review confirmed `Navbar.css` now adds a final mobile Web touch-target guard for navbar action icons, auth/account links, search controls, suggestion chips, and category rail buttons before the existing native App-specific `!important` closure. Targeted source scans and `git diff --check` passed for the touched files. No Maven/Jest/build/UI smoke/screenshot/commit was run per instruction.
- Non-issues recorded: Pet Gallery and Browsing History also contain earlier compact mobile rules, but their file-end touch-target guards already override those rules to 44px for actual controls. Desktop navbar sizing and decorative badges/amount labels remain unchanged.
- Resolution: Regular mobile Web navigation controls now keep commercial-grade tap targets without relying on the native App body class.

### F537: Storefront order and payment status labels lacked raw fallback

- Area: Frontend / Storefront order tracking, checkout payment, and profile order/payment i18n readability
- Environment: `frontend/src/pages/OrderTracking.tsx`, `frontend/src/pages/Checkout.tsx`, `frontend/src/pages/Profile.tsx`, customer order summary status tags, checkout payment card, profile order cards, order detail modal, payment detail modal, and payment history
- Steps: Open Track Order, Checkout payment result, or Profile Orders with an order/payment status outside the currently known localized status set, or with lowercase variants of a known status.
- Expected: Known customer-visible order/payment statuses should display localized labels, blank values should show the shared unknown label, and unexpected backend values should fall back to the raw value with neutral colors so customers and support can see the exact state.
- Actual: Storefront order/payment status tags rendered `t(\`status.${value}\`)` directly. Unknown statuses would be humanized into English-style labels instead of preserving the exact backend status, and some unknown values inherited non-neutral colors.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static source review at 2026-06-01 22:18 UTC found direct dynamic `status.*` translations in Order Tracking, Checkout, and Profile status tags.
- Retest: 2026-06-01 22:18 UTC static source review confirmed these storefront pages now route visible order/payment status tags through guarded helpers. Known statuses localize after normalization, blank statuses use `common.unknown`, and unexpected statuses fall back to raw display with default colors. Targeted dynamic-status scans, locale JSON parsing, and `git diff --check` passed for touched files. No Maven/Jest/build/UI smoke/screenshot/commit was run per instruction.
- Non-issues recorded: Raw order/payment status codes remain unchanged in comparisons, filters, payment polling, recovery logic, order actions, and API payloads because those are backend contract values. Pet Finder dynamic locale keys remain fixed front-end option unions rather than backend enum display.
- Resolution: Customer-facing order and payment status labels no longer hide unexpected backend values behind synthesized labels.

### F536: Admin order and payment status labels lacked raw fallback

Unknown values could therefore be shown as synthesized English-style labels instead of the exact backend status.

- Retest: 2026-06-01 22:12 UTC static source review confirmed Order Management now routes order and payment status tags through guarded helpers, Admin Dashboard now uses guarded order status helpers, known lowercase variants normalize before localization/color lookup, blank statuses use `common.unknown`, and unexpected statuses fall back to raw display with default colors. Targeted dynamic-status scans, locale JSON parsing, and `git diff --check` passed for touched files. No Maven/Jest/build/UI smoke/screenshot/commit was run per instruction.
- Non-issues recorded: Raw status codes remain unchanged in filters, quick links, API payloads, transition checks, payment sync eligibility, React keys, and dashboard aggregation keys because those are backend contract or routing values.
- Resolution: Admin operators no longer lose unexpected order/payment state codes behind synthesized labels in Orders and Dashboard views.

### F534: IP Blacklist status and source labels could mislead on unexpected values

- Area: Frontend / Admin IP Blacklist i18n and operator readability
- Environment: `frontend/src/pages/IpBlacklistManagement.tsx`, IP blacklist table status/source tags, release confirmation copy, and filter labels
- Steps: Open IP Blacklist Management with an entry status or source value outside the currently known `BLOCKED`/`MONITORING`/`RELEASED` and `LOGIN`/`PAYMENT`/`MANUAL` sets, or with lowercase variants from backend data.
- Expected: Known values should display localized labels with correct colors, blank values should display the common unknown label, and unexpected backend values should fall back to the raw value with a neutral color.
- Actual: The page directly built dynamic translation keys for all status/source values. Unknown values could be humanized into English-style labels, unknown statuses were colored as released/green, and unknown sources were colored as manual/purple.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static source review at 2026-06-01 22:00 UTC found `getStatusLabel` and `getSourceLabel` returned dynamic `t(...)` values without raw fallback, while `statusColor` returned green for any non-`BLOCKED`/`MONITORING` value and `sourceColor` returned purple for any non-`LOGIN`/`PAYMENT` value.
- Retest: 2026-06-01 22:00 UTC static source review confirmed IP Blacklist now normalizes known statuses/sources before localization and color selection, uses `common.unknown` for blank values, falls back to raw unexpected values, and uses neutral tag color for unknown status/source values. Targeted source scans and `git diff --check` passed for the touched file. No Maven/Jest/build/UI smoke/screenshot/commit was run per instruction.
- Non-issues recorded: Filter options remain limited to the backend-supported `ALL`/`BLOCKED`/`MONITORING`/`RELEASED` and `ALL`/`LOGIN`/`PAYMENT`/`MANUAL` sets; raw fallback applies to displayed data values outside those sets.
- Resolution: IP Blacklist operators no longer see unexpected blacklist states or sources hidden behind misleading colors or synthesized labels.

### F533: i18n defaultValue fallback was ignored for dynamic admin labels

- Area: Frontend / Shared i18n fallback and Product Management operator readability
- Environment: `frontend/src/i18n.tsx`, `frontend/src/pages/ProductManagement.tsx`, dynamic product listing issue labels, URL import warnings, product import statuses, and import history actions
- Steps: In Product Management, display a listing-quality issue, URL import warning, import status, or import history action value that does not yet have a locale entry, while the page calls `t(dynamicKey, { defaultValue: rawValue })`.
- Expected: Missing dynamic translation keys should display the supplied raw backend/diagnostic value from `defaultValue`, preserving the exact state/action for operators.
- Actual: The local `translate(...)` implementation treated `defaultValue` as a normal interpolation parameter and still fell back to `humanizeKey(key)` when the translation was missing. Product Management's existing raw fallbacks therefore did not actually work for unknown dynamic values.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static source review at 2026-06-01 21:54 UTC found the only `defaultValue` translation calls in `ProductManagement.tsx`, while `frontend/src/i18n.tsx` selected `humanizeKey(key)` whenever both localized and English fallback strings were missing.
- Retest: 2026-06-01 21:54 UTC static source review confirmed `translate(...)` now recognizes `params.defaultValue` as the missing-key fallback and excludes `defaultValue` from ordinary template interpolation. Targeted `defaultValue` usage scans, locale JSON parsing, and `git diff --check` passed for touched files. No Maven/Jest/build/UI smoke/screenshot/commit was run per instruction.
- Non-issues recorded: Existing humanized-key fallback remains for translation calls that do not explicitly provide `defaultValue`; this preserves current behavior for generic missing keys.
- Resolution: Product Management dynamic diagnostics and import labels now honor raw fallback values when backend/internal enums expand before locale files are updated.

### F530: Customer support order cards and workflows did not handle refund-stage statuses

- Area: Frontend / Customer support widget order-context i18n and after-sales workflow
- Environment: `frontend/src/components/CustomerSupportWidget.tsx`, `frontend/src/utils/supportWorkflow.ts`, shared order messages, support recommended actions
- Steps: Share or load an order in the support widget while the order status is `RETURN_REFUNDING`, `RETURNED`, `REFUNDED`, or another backend value outside the current support workflow templates, then inspect the order card status chip and recommended support actions in Chinese or Spanish.
- Expected: Known refund-stage statuses should display localized labels and provide specific refund/closure follow-up actions; unexpected statuses should fall back to the raw backend value instead of synthesized English-style labels.
- Actual: The support widget rendered order-card and workflow status labels with direct dynamic `t(\`status.${status}\`)` lookups, and the support workflow templates stopped at `RETURN_SHIPPED`/`CANCELLED`. Refund-processing and refunded orders therefore fell back to generic next-step support actions, and unknown statuses could be humanized into English-like copy in localized sessions.

- Evidence: Static source review at 2026-06-01 21:36 UTC found `CustomerSupportWidget.tsx` passed `t(\`status.${workflowOrder.status}\`)` into `buildSupportOrderWorkflowActions(...)` and rendered shared order cards with `t(\`status.${order.status}\`)`, while `supportWorkflow.ts` lacked `RETURN_REFUNDING`, `RETURNED`, and `REFUNDED` templates in English, Chinese, and Spanish.
- Retest: 2026-06-01 21:36 UTC static source review confirmed Customer Support now formats order statuses through a guarded `formatOrderStatusLabel(...)` helper that localizes known order states and falls back to raw unknown values, and support workflows now include refund-progress, refund-complete, and refund-record actions across English, Chinese, and Spanish. Targeted source scans and `git diff --check` passed for touched files. No Maven/Jest/build/UI smoke/screenshot/commit was run per instruction.
- Non-issues recorded: Payment method tags in support order cards remain raw because they are configured payment-channel/provider identifiers; support workflow fallback remains for truly unknown order states.
- Resolution: Support conversations now provide clearer localized after-sales guidance for refund-stage orders.

Localized sessions could fall back to English-style humanized `RETURN REFUNDING` text or default gray status tags, Admin Orders treated its next action as the generic completed fallback, the direct status filter omitted it, and Profile's after-sales helper could report no active return task when only refund-processing returns remained.

Locale JSON parsing, targeted key-presence checks, `RETURN_REFUNDING` source scans, and `git diff --check` passed for touched files. No Maven/Jest/build/UI smoke/screenshot/commit was run per instruction.
- Non-issues recorded: The backend return/refund state machine and mapper quick-filter semantics were left unchanged; the existing `REFUNDING` quick filter remains a combined refund-operations queue that can include payment refunding and return-refund processing.
- Resolution: Return-refund processing orders now stay readable and operationally visible across localized customer and admin order surfaces.

### F524: Brand and logistics-carrier status columns could mislabel unknown states

### F449: Customer confirm-receipt actions submitted without final confirmation

- Area: Frontend / Customer order lifecycle safety
- Environment: `frontend/src/pages/Profile.tsx` account order cards and `frontend/src/pages/OrderTracking.tsx` public/guest order tracking
- Steps: Open a shipped order from the signed-in profile page or order tracking page, then click `Confirm receipt` / localized equivalent.
- Expected: Confirming receipt should require a final confirmation that identifies the order before marking it completed, because the action changes the order lifecycle, affects fulfillment metrics, and can influence after-sales handling.
- Actual: The profile order card called `handleConfirmReceipt(order.id)` directly, and the order tracking page called `confirmReceipt` directly from the shipped-order primary button. One accidental tap could mark the order `COMPLETED` without a final review step, while neighboring cancellation and admin lifecycle operations already require confirmation.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static source review at 2026-06-01 13:30 UTC found both customer-facing shipped-order `Confirm receipt` buttons directly invoked the status-changing API path through `orderApi.confirm(...)`.
- Retest: 2026-06-01 13:30 UTC static source review confirmed both Profile and OrderTracking now open a localized `Modal.confirm` showing the order number/id before running the existing confirm-receipt mutation. English, Spanish, and Chinese confirmation copy was added. No Maven/Jest/build/UI smoke/screenshot/commit was run per instruction.
- Resolution: Customer and guest confirm-receipt actions now require explicit final confirmation before completing an order.

Later mobile rules usually restored 44px at wider breakpoints, but there was no App-specific bottom-navigation closure equivalent to the header icon 44px guard, so native ultra-narrow WebViews could still inherit the sub-44px target.

No Maven/Jest/build/UI smoke/screenshot/commit was run per instruction.
- Resolution: Native App bottom navigation now preserves commercial mobile touch targets on ultra-narrow WebView screens.

### F447: Admin order payment sync buttons had no confirmation

- Area: Frontend / Admin order payment operation safety
- Environment: `frontend/src/pages/OrderManagement.tsx`, order detail and refund payment-history sync actions
- Steps: Open an order's payment history or refund modal payment evidence table and click `Sync status` for a pending payment.
- Expected: Payment gateway synchronization should require confirmation before contacting the gateway and potentially changing payment/order/refund state, because it affects fulfillment and refund decisions.
- Actual: Both payment-history sync buttons called `handleSyncPayment(...)` directly from `onClick`, so one accidental click could trigger a gateway sync and update payment status without a final review step. Other high-impact order operations already use confirmation modals.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static source review at 2026-06-01 13:14 UTC found the detail and refund payment tables directly wired pending-payment sync buttons to `handleSyncPayment(payment, 'detail' | 'refund')`.
- Retest: 2026-06-01 13:14 UTC static source review confirmed both payment sync buttons are now wrapped in localized `Popconfirm` prompts showing the payment id before `handleSyncPayment(...)` runs. No Maven/Jest/build/UI smoke/screenshot/commit was run per instruction.
- Resolution: Admin payment status synchronization now requires explicit confirmation before contacting the gateway and mutating payment state.

### F446: Admin order page summary missed the missing-tracking quick count

- Area: Backend / Admin order management page summary
- Environment: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/AdminController.java`, `/admin/orders/page` summary payload
- Steps: Open Admin Orders and rely on the Missing tracking quick card/count in the order-management summary while the table can be filtered with `quick=MISSING_TRACKING`.
- Expected: The page summary should expose `MISSING_TRACKING` using the same quick-filter counting path as the order table so the card count matches the rows operators will drill into.
- Actual: `buildAdminOrdersPage(...)` returned only `orderService.countAdminOrderSummary(safeSearch)` as `summary`. In the controller contract tested by QA, `MISSING_TRACKING` was absent/null instead of being populated from `countAdminOrders(null, safeSearch, "MISSING_TRACKING")`, so the quick card could fall back to current-page rows or show a stale/missing count.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static source review at 2026-06-01 13:11 UTC matched cron failure `AdminControllerOrderPageTest.orderPageSummaryIncludesMissingTrackingQuickFilter` and found the page response did not explicitly add the quick-filter count to the summary map.
- Retest: 2026-06-01 13:11 UTC static source review confirmed `buildAdminOrdersPage(...)` now builds the summary through a helper that preserves the base summary and overwrites `MISSING_TRACKING` with `orderService.countAdminOrders(null, safeSearch, "MISSING_TRACKING")`. No Maven/Jest/build/UI smoke/screenshot/commit was run per instruction.
- Resolution: Admin Orders summary now includes the missing-tracking quick-filter count from the same service path used by the table filter.

### F445: Security audit summary result cards ignored active result filters

- Area: Backend / Admin security audit log summary
- Environment: `src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/SecurityAuditLogService.java`, `/admin/audit-logs/summary`
- Steps: Open Security Audit Logs, apply active list filters such as action `REFUND_COMPLETE`, result `FAILURE`, actor `admin`, resource `PAYMENT`, and request the summary.
- Expected: The summary count cards and grouping panels should use the same active filters as the audit-log list so operators see metrics for the exact slice they are investigating.
- Actual: `summary(...)` always forced separate `SUCCESS` and `FAILURE` result counts even when a result filter was already active. When the active result filter conflicted with the forced result, the service returned `0` without issuing the same filtered count query, so the success/failure cards could diverge from the currently filtered audit-log list and the backend summary contract tested by QA.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static source review at 2026-06-01 13:10 UTC matched cron failure `SecurityAuditLogServiceTest.summaryAppliesActiveListFiltersToCountsAndGroups` and found `count("SUCCESS" | "FAILURE", ..., resultFilter, ...)` bypassed the current result-filter query path for conflicting result filters.
- Retest: 2026-06-01 13:17 UTC static source review after cron run #162 confirmed summary success/failure counts now only force `SUCCESS` / `FAILURE` when no result filter is active, and the generated summary SQL binds active list filters before the time range so `SecurityAuditLogServiceTest.summaryAppliesActiveListFiltersToCountsAndGroups` sees the expected action/result/actor/resource/start/end parameter order. No Maven/Jest/build/UI smoke/screenshot/commit was run per instruction.
- Resolution: Security audit summary cards now stay scoped to the active audit-log list filters instead of mixing filtered totals with unfiltered forced-result counts.

### F441: Admin direct coupon grant submitted without final confirmation

- Area: Frontend / Admin Coupon Management direct issuance safety
- Environment: `frontend/src/pages/CouponManagement.tsx`, direct coupon grant modal
- Steps: In Coupon Management, choose a coupon, open `Grant`, select one or more users, and press the modal OK button.
- Expected: Direct coupon grants should show a final confirmation with the coupon and recipient count before sending coupons into user wallets, because this action creates customer-facing discounts and consumes promotion budget.
- Actual: The modal OK handler called `adminApi.grantCoupon(...)` immediately after form validation, so one accidental OK click could grant the selected coupon to all chosen users without a final review step.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static source review at 2026-06-01 12:54 UTC found `submitGrant` validated `userIds` and then called `adminApi.grantCoupon(...)` directly.
- Retest: 2026-06-01 12:54 UTC static source review confirmed direct grants now open a localized `Modal.confirm` showing the coupon name and selected user count before calling `adminApi.grantCoupon(...)`. No Maven/Jest/build/UI smoke/screenshot/commit was run per instruction.
- Resolution: Manual direct coupon grants now require explicit final confirmation before customer discounts are issued.

### F440: Admin refund modal allowed refunds without an audit reason

- Area: Frontend / Admin Order Management refund audit safety
- Environment: `frontend/src/pages/OrderManagement.tsx`, refund modal and `handleRefundOrder`
- Steps: In Order Management, open `Refund now` for a refundable order, clear the refund reason field, and submit the refund.
- Expected: Admin refunds should require a non-empty reason before submitting so finance, audit, and support teams have a traceable explanation for why customer funds were returned.
- Actual: The refund modal displayed an optional reason textarea and `handleRefundOrder` sent `reason: refundReason.trim()` even when it was empty, allowing high-impact refunds without an audit note.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static source review at 2026-06-01 12:45 UTC found `handleRefundOrder` did not validate `refundReason` before calling `adminApi.refundOrder(...)`.
- Retest: 2026-06-01 12:45 UTC static source review confirmed refund submission now stops with localized English, Spanish, and Chinese warning copy when the reason is blank, and the refund reason textarea shows an error state until a non-empty reason is entered. No Maven/Jest/build/UI smoke/screenshot/commit was run per instruction.
- Resolution: Admin refunds now require an explicit audit/support reason before money-returning actions can be submitted.

### F439: Admin pet birthday coupon manual issuing had no confirmation

- Area: Frontend / Admin Coupon Management marketing issuance safety
- Environment: `frontend/src/pages/CouponManagement.tsx`, pet birthday coupon automation header action
