Backend `/admin/questions` still returns `productQuestionService.getAdminQueue(status, limit)` at lines 2353-2357, while `/admin/questions/summary` still returns the global `productQuestionService.adminSummary()` at lines 2360-2362 and `ProductQuestionServiceImpl.adminSummary()` counts all/answered/unanswered/stale rows independently at lines 65-75.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 10:08 UTC static retest confirmed F212 is FIXED in current source. `AdminController.getAdminQuestions(...)` and `getAdminQuestionSummary(...)` both accept `status` and `search`; `ProductQuestionServiceImpl.adminSummary(status, search)` uses scoped repository counts; `adminApi.getQuestionSummary(...)` and `getQuestions(...)` share normalized status/search params; and `ProductQuestionManagement` renders the returned scoped summary over the same backend queue, with `visibleQuestions = questions` instead of client-filtering only the loaded rows. No backend compile, frontend build, or automated UI test was run per instruction.

### F211: Non-super admins can change privileged operators' login and recovery contact fields

- Area: Backend / AdminController user profile authorization; Frontend / UserManagement profile modal; Backend / email login and forgot-password identity fields
- Environment: `/admin/users`, `PUT /admin/users/{id}`, user-management profile edit action, standard admins with the `users` page permission, and target users whose `role` or `roleCode` is `ADMIN` / `SUPER_ADMIN`
- Steps: Log in as a standard admin with access to user management, open another admin or super-admin row, edit its email and/or phone in the profile modal, then use email login or forgot-password flows with the updated contact value.
- Expected: Changing login and recovery identifiers for privileged operator accounts should require super-admin-grade backend authorization, or a separate audited privileged-account control. Standard admins should not be able to redirect another admin/super-admin account's email-code login or password-reset address.
- Actual: `UserManagement.tsx` renders the profile edit button for every row and sends `email`, `phone`, and `address` through `adminApi.updateUser(...)` without checking `canManageRoles` or target privilege. `AdminController.updateUser()` only requires super-admin authority for the role branch; the generic contact branch copies `email`, `phone`, and `address` onto the target account and persists them for any caller with `/admin/users` access. Those fields are not passive profile data: `UserMapper.findByUsernameOrPhoneOrEmail()` uses email and phone as login identifiers, `EmailLoginService.sendLoginCode()` sends codes to the stored email account, and `AuthController.forgotPassword()` plus `UserService.resetPassword()` accept password reset once the verified email/login match the stored values. A standard admin can therefore redirect another privileged operator's login/recovery contact path even though the same controller blocks standard admins from changing roles or deleting privileged accounts.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 12:16 UTC found `frontend/src/pages/UserManagement.tsx` always rendering `<Button ... onClick={() => openProfileModal(record)}>` and `handleProfileSubmit()` calling `adminApi.updateUser(editingUser.id, { email, phone, address })`. Backend `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/AdminController.java` has `SecurityUtils.assertSuperAdmin(authentication)` only under `if (user.getRole() != null)`, while `if (user.getEmail() != null) existing.setEmail(...)`, `if (user.getPhone() != null) existing.setPhone(...)`, and `if (user.getAddress() != null) existing.setAddress(...)` run without target-role or caller super-admin checks. `src/main/resources/mapper/UserMapper.xml` matches login by username, phone, normalized phone, or email, and `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/AuthController.java` verifies forgot-password requests against the stored email before `UserService.resetPassword(...)` updates the password.
- Evidence: Development handling at 2026-05-30 22:23 UTC added backend privileged-target checks in `AdminController.updateUser()`: non-super admins cannot change email, phone, or address for other admin/custom-admin/super-admin operators. The user-management profile edit button is also disabled for privileged target rows unless the current operator is super admin or editing self. No project tests/builds were run per instruction; static `git diff --check` and source grep were used.

### F210: Non-super admins can ban other admin or super-admin accounts through user status updates

- Area: Backend / AdminController user status authorization; Frontend / UserManagement status actions; Backend / admin permission model
- Environment: `/admin/users`, `PUT /admin/users/{id}`, built-in `ADMIN` users with the `users` page permission, and target users whose `role` or `roleCode` is `ADMIN` / `SUPER_ADMIN`
- Steps: Log in as a non-super admin who has access to user management, select another admin or super-admin user, or send a direct `PUT /admin/users/{targetAdminId}` request with JSON body `{"status":"BANNED"}`.
- Expected: Disabling admin or super-admin accounts should require the same elevated backend protection as changing their roles or deleting them. A standard admin should not be able to ban another privileged operator unless explicitly granted a super-admin-grade account-control permission.
- Actual: `AdminController.updateUser()` requires only `/admin/users` access for status changes. Its role branch calls `SecurityUtils.assertSuperAdmin(authentication)`, and `deleteUser()` requires super admin before deleting an existing `ADMIN` or `SUPER_ADMIN`, but the `user.getStatus() != null` branch only normalizes and persists `ACTIVE`, `BANNED`, or `GUEST`. The `UserManagement.tsx` status button is disabled only for the current row's own account (`isSelf`) and is not gated by `canManageRoles`, so standard admins with the users page can ban or unban other privileged operators even though they cannot edit roles or delete those same accounts.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 12:08 UTC found `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/AdminController.java` class-level `@PreAuthorize("hasRole('ADMIN')")` and `AdminRoleService.PATH_PERMISSIONS` mapping `/admin/users` to the `users` permission. In `updateUser()`, `SecurityUtils.assertSuperAdmin(authentication)` is used only when `user.getRole() != null`; the status branch accepts `normalizeUserStatus(...)` and calls `existing.setStatus(normalizedStatus)` without checking `existing.getRole()` or the caller's super-admin authority. The same controller's `deleteUser()` explicitly blocks non-super admins from deleting existing admin/super-admin users. Frontend `frontend/src/pages/UserManagement.tsx` computes `canManageRoles` for role selects, but the ban/unban button only uses `disabled={isSelf}` before calling `adminApi.updateUser(user.id, { status: newStatus })`.
- Evidence: Development handling at 2026-05-30 22:23 UTC made `AdminController.updateUser()` require `SecurityUtils.assertSuperAdmin(authentication)` before changing status on admin/custom-admin/super-admin target accounts. `UserManagement.tsx` mirrors the backend by disabling the status action for privileged target rows when the current operator is not super admin. No project tests/builds were run per instruction; static `git diff --check` and source grep were used.

### F209: Admin user status updates can ban the current operator through the backend endpoint

- Area: Backend / AdminController user status updates; Frontend / UserManagement self-protection; Backend / JWT authentication enabled checks
- Environment: `/admin/users`, `PUT /admin/users/{id}`, current admin/super-admin operator, and JWT-authenticated admin requests
- Steps: Log in as an admin or super admin, identify the current operator id from `/users/profile`, then send a direct `PUT /admin/users/{currentId}` request with JSON body `{"status":"BANNED"}` instead of using the disabled UI button.
- Expected: Backend should reject attempts to ban, disable, or otherwise change the current operator's own account status, matching the existing backend protections for current-operator role changes and deletion. Frontend self-protection should be defense in depth, not the only guard.
- Actual: `UserManagement.tsx` disables the ban/unban button for `record.id === currentUserId`, but `AdminController.updateUser()` only blocks current-operator changes in the role branch and delete endpoint. The status branch normalizes `ACTIVE`, `BANNED`, or `GUEST`, assigns `existing.setStatus(normalizedStatus)`, and persists through `userService.update(existing)` without checking whether `id` is the authenticated operator. Because `JwtAuthenticationFilter` only authenticates a token when the freshly loaded `UserDetails` is enabled, and `UserDetailsImpl.isEnabled()` returns false for `BANNED`, a direct backend request can self-ban the active operator and cause subsequent authenticated admin requests with that account to fail.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 11:56 UTC found `frontend/src/pages/UserManagement.tsx` computing `const isSelf = record.id === currentUserId` and disabling the status button, while `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/AdminController.java` protects current-operator role assignment (`Cannot change current operator role`) and delete (`Cannot delete current operator`) but not `user.getStatus() != null` updates. The same controller's `normalizeUserStatus()` allows `ACTIVE`, `BANNED`, and `GUEST`. `src/main/java/com/example/shop/security/UserDetailsImpl.java` returns false from `isEnabled()` when status is `BANNED`, and `src/main/java/com/example/shop/security/JwtAuthenticationFilter.java` requires `userDetails.isEnabled()` before installing the JWT authentication.
- Evidence: Development handling at 2026-05-30 22:23 UTC added a backend self-status guard in `AdminController.updateUser()`. Direct `PUT /admin/users/{currentId}` status changes now return a controlled bad request and audit failure with `Cannot change current operator status`, matching the existing frontend disabled state and backend self-role/delete guards. No project tests/builds were run per instruction; static `git diff --check` and source grep were used.

### F208: Config center reloads masked secrets into the publish editor and blocks unrelated changes

- Area: Backend / ConfigCenterService sensitive-value masking and validation; Frontend / ConfigCenter editor workflow
- Environment: `/admin/config-center`, `GET /admin/config-center`, `POST /admin/config-center/publish`, `POST /admin/config-center/apply`, and configs containing allowed sensitive keys such as `payment.callback-secret`
- Steps: Load a Nacos properties file that contains a sensitive allowed key such as `payment.callback-secret=super-secret-value-1234567890`, change only an unrelated non-sensitive value in the config-center editor, then click Apply runtime or Publish without re-entering/removing the secret value.
- Expected: Operators should be able to make unrelated non-sensitive config changes without needing to know or replace existing secrets. If secrets are masked on read, the editor should preserve the original secret server-side or provide a clear redacted-value placeholder contract that does not submit `****` as a new value.
- Actual: `ConfigCenterService.buildResponse()` masks sensitive keys in `response.content`, and `ConfigCenter.tsx` writes that masked `response.data.content` directly into the editable textarea. On apply/publish, the frontend submits the textarea content back unchanged, and `ConfigCenterService.validateParsedProperties(..., publishing=true)` rejects any sensitive key whose value contains `****` with `Sensitive key ... still contains a masked placeholder`. As a result, an operator who only edits `order.default-shipping-fee` while leaving the displayed masked `payment.callback-secret=su****90` line in place cannot apply or publish the unrelated change.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 11:43 UTC found `frontend/src/pages/ConfigCenter.tsx` calling `form.setFieldsValue({ content: response.data.content })` after both snapshot load and publish, while backend `src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/ConfigCenterService.java` sets `response.setContent(maskSensitiveContent(content, parsed))`. The same service marks keys matching password/secret/token patterns as sensitive, masks them to values containing `****`, and then `validateParsedProperties(...)` adds an error when `publishing && isSensitive(key) && looksMasked(value)`. `src/test/java/com/example/shop/service/ConfigCenterServiceTest.java` explicitly covers both sides: `applyAllowedRuntimePropertiesAndMasksSensitiveValuesInResponse()` verifies sensitive values are masked in the response, and `rejectsMaskedSensitivePlaceholderOnPublishOrApply()` verifies `payment.callback-secret=su****90` is rejected.
- Evidence: Development handling at 2026-05-30 22:49 UTC changed `ConfigCenterService.publishInternal(...)` so masked sensitive placeholders are resolved server-side from the current Nacos config before validation, publish, or runtime apply. The backend still refuses masked placeholders when the original Nacos value cannot be read or is itself masked, but unrelated non-sensitive edits now preserve existing secret values without exposing them to the frontend or writing `****` back. `ConfigCenterServiceTest` was updated to cover both preserve-from-Nacos and cannot-preserve rejection contracts. Static `git diff --check` passed; frontend `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed before the final CSS-only App style pass.

### F207: Admin user contact edits bypass customer email/phone validation and uniqueness checks

- Area: Backend / AdminController user profile updates; Frontend / UserManagement profile edit form; Backend / UserService contact validation
- Environment: `/admin/users`, `PUT /admin/users/{id}`, user-management profile modal, and `users.email` / `users.phone` account identifiers
- Steps: As an admin, edit another user's profile from user management and submit a malformed or already-used email/phone value, such as an email already owned by another account or a phone number longer/differently formatted than the login-normalization rules allow.
- Expected: Admin-side contact edits should enforce the same email format, lowercase normalization, phone normalization/length limit, and duplicate email/phone checks as customer self-service profile updates, and validation failures should be returned as controlled audited failures.
- Actual: `UserManagement.tsx` only applies light frontend checks (`email` type, email max 120, phone max 40) and sends raw trimmed values through `adminApi.updateUser(...)`. `AdminController.updateUser()` copies `email`, `phone`, and `address` directly onto the existing `User` and calls `userService.update(existing)`. That generic update path only sets `updatedAt` and delegates to `UserMapper.update`, bypassing `UserService.updateProfileContact(...)`, which is the path that lowercases email, validates format, normalizes phone to the 20-character users column limit, and rejects duplicate email/phone ownership. Direct API callers can therefore store invalid contact values when DB constraints permit them, or trigger raw unique/length constraint failures outside the controller's audited validation branches.
- Status: FIXED
- Fix: 2026-05-31 10:25 UTC development pass tightened admin user profile edits end-to-end. Backend generic user update now enforces email format/lowercase normalization, phone normalization/length, address length normalization, and duplicate email/phone checks; the user-management phone field now matches the backend 20-character phone limit.
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 11:39 UTC found `frontend/src/pages/UserManagement.tsx` profile modal accepting email max 120 and phone max 40 before calling `adminApi.updateUser(editingUser.id, { email, phone, address })`. Backend `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/AdminController.java` handles `PUT /admin/users/{id}` by assigning `existing.setEmail(user.getEmail())`, `existing.setPhone(user.getPhone())`, and then calling `userService.update(existing)`. `src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/UserService.java` shows `update()` only setting `updatedAt` and calling `userMapper.update(user)`, while the validated contact path `updateProfileContact()` normalizes/lowercases email, calls `assertValidEmail(...)`, normalizes optional phone to `USER_PHONE_MAX_CHARS = 20`, checks `findByUsernameOrPhoneOrEmail(...)` / `findByPhone(...)` for duplicates, and finally writes through `updateProfileContact(...)`. `src/main/resources/mapper/UserMapper.xml` writes non-null `email` and `phone` directly in the generic `update` statement, so the admin edit path skips the validated contact contract.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 10:00 UTC static retest confirmed the admin contact validation gap remains. `AdminController.updateUser()` now has additional action-permission and privileged-target guards, but the contact branch still assigns `existing.setEmail(user.getEmail())`, `existing.setPhone(user.getPhone())`, and `existing.setAddress(user.getAddress())` at lines 1050-1057 before calling `userService.update(existing)` at line 1059. That `UserService.update()` method still only sets `updatedAt` and delegates to `userMapper.update(user)` at lines 162-166, while the validated `updateProfileContact()` path with lowercasing, email format validation, phone normalization, and duplicate checks remains separate at lines 168-191. `UserManagement` still submits trimmed email/phone/address through `adminApi.updateUser(...)` at lines 232-245, with only frontend-light constraints.

### F206: Payment provider/config failures can incorrectly escalate client IPs to the payment blacklist

- Area: Backend / PaymentController failure handling; Backend / IpBlacklistService payment-failure escalation
- Environment: `/payments`, `/payments/{id}/sync`, `/payments/callback`, `/payments/stripe/webhook`, generic/Stripe payment channels, and `security.ip-blacklist.payment-failure-threshold`
- Steps: Trigger repeated payment operations while a server-side provider dependency is misconfigured or failing, such as missing Stripe secret key, missing generic create URL, gateway 5xx/non-2xx, invalid gateway response, disabled payment simulation, missing production callback secret, or missing Stripe webhook secret, then inspect `ip_blacklist_entries` for `source='PAYMENT'`.
- Expected: Only client-controlled suspicious payment access failures, such as invalid guest credentials, invalid callback signatures, or tampered callback payloads, should contribute to IP blacklist thresholds. Provider outages and backend configuration errors should be logged/audited/alerted without blocking the customer's IP or webhook source.
- Actual: `PaymentController` catches broad `IllegalArgumentException | IllegalStateException` failures from create, sync, simulate, callback, and Stripe webhook paths and calls `ipBlacklistService.recordPaymentFailure(...)` for all of them. `PaymentService` throws `IllegalStateException` for server/provider conditions including missing Stripe secret key, missing generic payment URL, gateway create failures, invalid/empty gateway responses, disabled simulation, missing production callback secret, missing Stripe webhook secret, and Stripe payment state update failures. After the configured threshold, `IpBlacklistService.recordFailure(SOURCE_PAYMENT, ...)` transitions the caller IP to `BLOCKED`, so repeated legitimate checkout attempts during an outage can lock out customers or payment webhook infrastructure.
- Status: FIXED
- Fix: 2026-05-31 12:28 UTC static verification confirmed `PaymentController` now routes failure recording through `recordPaymentBlacklistFailureIfSuspicious(...)`. The helper records only `IllegalArgumentException` and 4xx `ResponseStatusException` failures, while broad `IllegalStateException` provider/config/outage failures no longer increment payment blacklist counters.
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 11:26 UTC found `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/PaymentController.java` calling `ipBlacklistService.recordPaymentFailure(...)` in catch blocks for `createPayment()`, `simulatePaid()`, `simulateCallback()`, `syncPayment()`, `callback()`, and `stripeWebhook()`, including the Stripe webhook `IllegalStateException` path that returns 500 `Payment provider is temporarily unavailable`. `src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/PaymentService.java` throws `IllegalStateException` for server/provider faults such as `Stripe secret key is not configured`, `Create payment URL is not configured`, `Gateway create payment failed`, `Gateway create payment request failed`, invalid gateway responses, `Stripe webhook secret is not configured`, `Payment simulation is disabled`, and missing production callback secret. `IpBlacklistService.recordPaymentFailure()` records `SOURCE_PAYMENT`, uses `paymentFailureThreshold()` from runtime config, and `recordFailure()` sets status to `BLOCKED` once the threshold is reached. Default config sets `security.ip-blacklist.payment-failure-threshold=5` and protects `/payment` and `/payments` paths.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 10:07 UTC static retest confirmed F206 remains OPEN. `PaymentController.createPayment()`, `simulatePaid()`, `simulateCallback()`, `syncPayment()`, `callback()`, and `stripeWebhook()` still call `ipBlacklistService.recordPaymentFailure(...)` from broad `IllegalArgumentException` / `IllegalStateException` catch paths at lines 75-83, 99-107, 123-131, 147-155, 171-175, and 193-200. `PaymentService` still throws `IllegalStateException` for server/provider conditions such as missing Stripe secret, generic gateway URL/config failures, gateway non-2xx or invalid responses, disabled payment simulation, and missing production callback or webhook secrets. The controller still does not distinguish client-controlled suspicious failures from provider/config outages before escalating the caller IP.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 12:28 UTC static retest found every payment catch path calls `recordPaymentBlacklistFailureIfSuspicious(...)`; `shouldRecordPaymentBlacklistFailure(...)` returns true for `IllegalArgumentException` and 4xx `ResponseStatusException`, and false for `IllegalStateException`, so provider/config failures remain audited without blacklist escalation.

### F205: Customer and guest confirm-receipt mutations bypass order audit logging

- Area: Backend / OrderController customer and guest order lifecycle audit coverage
- Environment: `PUT /orders/{id}/confirm`, `PUT /orders/guest/{id}/confirm`, shipped customer/guest orders, and security audit logs
- Steps: Confirm receipt for a shipped order from the profile page or guest order tracking flow, then inspect audit logs for an order lifecycle event covering the status change to `COMPLETED`.
- Expected: Customer-facing order lifecycle mutations that change order status should have security/audit coverage comparable to cancellation, return request, return shipment submission, and admin order status updates, including success and failure outcomes where possible.
- Actual: `OrderController.confirmReceiptInternal()` verifies ownership or guest credentials and calls `orderService.updateOrderStatus(id, "COMPLETED")`, which advances valid `SHIPPED -> COMPLETED` orders and sends customer notifications. The method then returns `Order completed` without any `auditLogService.record(...)` call. Failure paths from invalid credentials, missing order, invalid transition, or update failure also do not create a confirm-receipt audit entry. In the same controller, cancellation, return request, and return-shipment paths explicitly record audit events, and admin status updates record `ORDER_STATUS_UPDATE`/status-specific events.
- Status: FIXED
- Fix: 2026-05-31 12:28 UTC static verification confirmed `OrderController.confirmReceiptInternal(...)` now records `ORDER_CONFIRM_RECEIPT` audit events for both success and failure, including authenticated and guest confirm-receipt paths.
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 11:19 UTC found `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/OrderController.java` routes both `/orders/{id}/confirm` and `/orders/guest/{id}/confirm` into `confirmReceiptInternal()`. That method resolves customer/guest access, calls `orderService.updateOrderStatus(id, "COMPLETED")`, and returns a success response without audit logging. `src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/OrderService.java` allows the `SHIPPED -> COMPLETED` transition in `assertNextStatus()` and `updateOrderStatus()` writes the status through `orderRepository.updateStatus(...)`. Nearby controller flows `cancelOrderInternal()`, `returnOrderInternal()`, and `submitReturnShipmentInternal()` do record `ORDER_CANCEL`, `RETURN_REQUEST`, and `RETURN_SHIPMENT_SUBMIT` audit events, so confirm receipt is the lifecycle mutation missing audit coverage.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 10:07 UTC static retest confirmed F205 remains OPEN. `/orders/{id}/confirm` and `/orders/guest/{id}/confirm` still delegate to `confirmReceiptInternal()` at lines 256-291. That method resolves authenticated/guest access, calls `orderService.updateOrderStatus(id, "COMPLETED")`, and returns `Order completed`, but it still has no `auditLogService.record(...)` call for either success or failure. Neighboring return request and return-shipment flows still record `RETURN_REQUEST` and `RETURN_SHIPMENT_SUBMIT` success/failure events at lines 315-340 and 358-393.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 12:28 UTC static retest confirmed `confirmReceiptInternal()` records `ORDER_CONFIRM_RECEIPT` with `SUCCESS` after `updateOrderStatus(..., "COMPLETED")`, and records `FAILURE` from the catch block with confirm-receipt metadata before rethrowing.

### F204: IP blacklist login-failure admin endpoint mutates blocks without audit logging

- Area: Backend / AdminIpBlacklistController audit coverage; Backend / IpBlacklistService login failure escalation
- Environment: `POST /admin/ip-blacklist/record-login-failure`, IP blacklist management, and security audit log review
- Steps: As an admin with access to IP blacklist operations, call `/admin/ip-blacklist/record-login-failure` repeatedly with an IP address/reason until the login-failure threshold is reached, then review security audit logs for the mutation.
- Expected: Any admin-accessible endpoint that records failures, changes IP blacklist state, or can escalate an IP to `BLOCKED` should create a security audit entry comparable to manual block/release and batch release operations.
- Actual: `AdminIpBlacklistController.recordLoginFailure()` is an `/admin/ip-blacklist` POST endpoint that calls `ipBlacklistService.recordFailure(SOURCE_LOGIN, ...)` and returns `204`, but it has no `Authentication`, `HttpServletRequest`, or `SecurityAuditLogService.record(...)` call. `IpBlacklistService.recordFailure()` inserts or updates `ip_blacklist_entries`, increments `failure_count`, can transition the row from `MONITORING` to `BLOCKED`, and emits a system alert when a block occurs. Those state changes therefore lack the actor/request audit trail present on manual block, single release, and batch release.
- Status: FIXED
- Fix: 2026-05-31 12:28 UTC static verification confirmed `/admin/ip-blacklist/record-login-failure` now accepts `Authentication` and `HttpServletRequest`, wraps the mutation in try/catch, requires the dedicated record-failure permission, and records `IP_BLACKLIST_RECORD_LOGIN_FAILURE` success/failure audit events.
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 11:17 UTC found `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/AdminIpBlacklistController.java` auditing `IP_BLACKLIST_BLOCK`, `IP_BLACKLIST_RELEASE`, and `IP_BLACKLIST_BATCH_RELEASE`, but `@PostMapping("/record-login-failure")` only calls `ipBlacklistService.recordFailure(...)`. `src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/IpBlacklistService.java` shows `recordFailure()` writing to `ip_blacklist_entries`, setting `status` to `BLOCKED` when `nextCount >= threshold`, updating `blocked_at`/`blocked_until`, and calling `recordBlockedAlert(...)`. No existing audit record is written for this admin-triggered mutation.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 10:11 UTC static retest confirmed F204 remains OPEN. `AdminIpBlacklistController.recordLoginFailure()` now requires `IP_BLACKLIST_RECORD_FAILURE_PERMISSION` at lines 114-119, but it still only calls `ipBlacklistService.recordFailure(...)` and returns `204`; it does not accept `HttpServletRequest`, wrap the mutation in try/catch, or call `auditLogService.record(...)` for success or failure. The neighboring manual block, release, and batch release endpoints still record `IP_BLACKLIST_BLOCK`, `IP_BLACKLIST_RELEASE`, and `IP_BLACKLIST_BATCH_RELEASE` events at lines 57-111, so the admin-triggered login-failure mutation remains unaudited.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 12:28 UTC static retest confirmed the endpoint records success after `ipBlacklistService.recordFailure(...)` and records failure in the catch block with the same metadata as other blacklist actions.

### F203: User management exposes two role editors whose effects conflict for custom-role users

- Area: Frontend / UserManagement role controls; Backend / AdminController user role update and AdminRoleService effective permissions
- Environment: `/admin/users` as a super admin, for a user whose `role='ADMIN'` and `roleCode` is a custom role such as `CUSTOMER_SERVICE`
- Steps: Open user management, find a custom-role admin user, use the legacy `role` dropdown to change the row to `ADMIN`, then reload permissions or inspect the row.
- Expected: The UI should expose one coherent role assignment control. If an operator chooses standard `ADMIN`, the user's effective role and permissions should become standard admin, or the UI should route all role changes through the `roleCode` assignment model.
- Actual: `UserManagement.tsx` renders both a `roleCode` select and a legacy `role` select for the same user row. The legacy role select calls `adminApi.updateUser(id, { role: newRole })`. Backend `AdminController.updateUser()` preserves the existing `roleCode` whenever the new role is non-USER and a role code already exists. `AdminRoleService.getPermissionsForUser()` prioritizes nonblank `roleCode` over `role`, so changing the legacy role to `ADMIN` for a custom-role user leaves the effective permissions on the old custom role even though the operator just used a visible ADMIN control.
- Status: FIXED
- Fix: 2026-05-31 10:25 UTC development pass removed the conflicting legacy role editor from `UserManagement` and made the remaining role-code selector the single role assignment surface, with a `USER` option that demotes through the legacy role API and admin/custom roles assigned through `/role-code`. Backend `AdminController.updateUser(... role=ADMIN ...)` now sets `roleCode=ADMIN` instead of preserving an old custom role code.
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 11:06 UTC found `frontend/src/pages/UserManagement.tsx` rendering editable selects for both `roleCode` and `role`, with `handleRoleChange()` calling `adminApi.updateUser(userId, { role: newRole })` and `handleRoleCodeChange()` calling `/admin/users/{id}/role-code`. Backend `AdminController.updateUser()` sets `roleCode = existing.getRoleCode()` and only replaces it when the new role is `USER`, `SUPER_ADMIN`, or the existing role code is blank. `AdminRoleService.getPermissionsForUser()` uses `roleCode` first, so the old custom role remains authoritative after a legacy role dropdown change.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 10:18 UTC static retest confirmed F203 remains OPEN. `UserManagement.tsx` still renders a role-code select at lines 282-303 and a separate legacy role select at lines 305-330. The role-code select calls `handleRoleCodeChange()` and `/admin/users/{id}/role-code`, while the legacy role select calls `handleRoleChange()` and `adminApi.updateUser(userId, { role: newRole })` at lines 156-174. Backend `AdminController.updateUser()` still initializes `roleCode` from `existing.getRoleCode()` and only clears/replaces it for `USER`, `SUPER_ADMIN`, or blank existing role code at lines 982-996, so a custom role code remains authoritative when the operator uses the visible `ADMIN` role dropdown.

### F202: Order summary quick cards are counted with the active status filter but clicks clear that filter

- Area: Frontend / OrderManagement summary cards and route filters; Backend / admin order summary counts
- Environment: `/admin/orders` with a status filter active, such as `status=CANCELLED`, and quick summary cards like SLA overdue, missing tracking, pending shipment, return shipped, or refunded
- Steps: Open order management with a status filter that excludes a quick queue, for example `CANCELLED`, observe that quick summary cards are counted under that status scope, then click a quick card such as `MISSING_TRACKING` or `REFUNDED`.
- Expected: A summary card's displayed count should match the rows opened when the card is clicked. If the count is scoped by the current status/search filters, clicking should preserve that scope or the UI should make clear that the click switches to a global quick queue.
- Actual: `AdminController.getOrdersPage()` calls `buildAdminOrderSummary(safeStatus, safeSearch)`, so quick-card counts are computed with the current `status` and `search` filters. However, `OrderManagement.handleQuickFilterChange()` sets `filterStatus(undefined)` and writes only `quick` to the route. The backend then loads the global quick queue without the status filter. Operators can therefore see a quick-card count of 0 under a status filter, click it, and land on a non-empty global quick-filter table, or see a small filtered count that opens many more rows.
- Status: FIXED
- Fix: 2026-05-31 12:28 UTC static verification confirmed backend quick summary counts now match the click destination scope. `AdminController` calls `buildAdminOrderSummary(safeSearch)`, and `buildAdminOrderSummary(...)` counts each quick queue with `status=null` plus the active search text, matching `OrderManagement.handleQuickFilterChange()` clearing status when opening a quick queue.
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 11:04 UTC found `AdminController.getOrdersPage()` returning `summary = buildAdminOrderSummary(safeStatus, safeSearch)`, and `buildAdminOrderSummary()` passing that status into `orderService.countAdminOrders(status, search, quick)` for each quick card. In `frontend/src/pages/OrderManagement.tsx`, `handleQuickFilterChange()` sets `setFilterStatus(undefined)` and `syncRouteFilters({ quick: nextQuick })`; `buildOrderFilterSearchParams()` then deletes `status` whenever `quick` is present. The card value and click destination therefore use different status scopes.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 10:34 UTC static retest confirmed F202 remains OPEN. `buildAdminOrdersPage()` still returns `summary = buildAdminOrderSummary(safeStatus, safeSearch)`, and `buildAdminOrderSummary()` still counts each quick queue through `orderService.countAdminOrders(status, search, quick)`. `OrderManagement.handleQuickFilterChange()` still clears `filterStatus`, and `buildOrderFilterSearchParams()` still deletes `status` when `quick` is present, so a status-scoped quick-card count opens an unscoped quick queue.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 12:28 UTC static retest found `getAllOrders()` now sets `summary` from `buildAdminOrderSummary(safeSearch)`, and the helper uses `orderService.countAdminOrders(null, search, quick)`, so status-filtered cards no longer disagree with the global quick queue opened by the UI.

### F201: Dashboard payment-method breakdown counts unpaid and cancelled orders

- Area: Backend / AdminController dashboard payment analytics; Frontend / AdminDashboard payment-method panel
- Environment: `/admin/dashboard` with orders that have `paymentMethod` set while still `PENDING_PAYMENT`, `CANCELLED`, `RETURNED`, or otherwise outside paid revenue statuses
- Steps: Create multiple checkout orders that choose a payment method but never complete payment, or cancel them before payment, then open the admin dashboard payment-method panel.
- Expected: Payment-method analytics beside revenue, paid-order, top-product, and sales-trend panels should use the same paid/commerce-completed status scope, or explicitly label itself as all attempted checkout payment-method selections.
- Actual: `AdminController.getDashboard()` builds `paymentMethodBreakdown` from every order whose `paymentMethod` is nonblank, without filtering to `revenueStatuses`. Order creation stores a payment method while the order is still `PENDING_PAYMENT`, and cancelled or refunded/returned orders can retain that method. The dashboard can therefore show payment-method share dominated by unpaid or cancelled checkout attempts, while adjacent revenue, top-products, and trend revenue use paid-status scopes.
- Status: FIXED
- Fix: 2026-05-31 12:28 UTC static verification confirmed `paymentMethodBreakdown` now uses `paidOrders.stream()` instead of the full `orders` list, aligning payment-method analytics with the surrounding paid/revenue status scope.
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 11:01 UTC found `AdminController.getDashboard()` defining `revenueStatuses` for paid-commerce metrics and using it for `paidOrders`, `salesTrend` revenue, and `topProducts`, but `paymentMethodBreakdown` only filters `order.getPaymentMethod() != null && !order.getPaymentMethod().isEmpty()`. `OrderService` sets `order.setStatus("PENDING_PAYMENT")` and stores `request.getPaymentMethod()` during checkout creation, so payment-method analytics include orders that have not paid.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 10:34 UTC static retest confirmed F201 remains OPEN. `AdminController.getDashboard()` still builds `paymentMethodBreakdown` from the full `orders` list with only a nonblank `paymentMethod` filter, while `paidOrders`, sales trend revenue, and top products continue to use the paid `revenueStatuses` scope. Unpaid and cancelled checkout attempts can still dominate the dashboard payment-method panel.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 12:28 UTC static retest confirmed `AdminController.getDashboard()` builds `paymentMethodBreakdown` from `paidOrders.stream()`, so unpaid/cancelled pending payment attempts no longer contribute to the chart.

### F200: Coupon management filters only the truncated recent list while summary counts all coupons

- Area: Frontend / CouponManagement filters and search; Backend / CouponService admin list and summary contract
- Environment: `/admin/coupons`, `admin.coupons.search-max-rows` default 500, and stores with more coupons than the configured admin list cap
- Steps: Create or keep more coupons than `admin.coupons.search-max-rows`, including older active/public/expiring/low-remaining coupons outside the newest capped list, then open coupon management and use status/scope/search filters.
- Expected: Coupon search and filters should run over the same dataset represented by the summary cards, or the UI should clearly disclose and paginate the capped result set so admins can reach older matching coupons.
- Actual: `CouponService.findAll()` returns only the newest `admin.coupons.search-max-rows` coupons with `PageRequest.of(0, limit, Sort.by(DESC, "id"))`. `CouponManagement.tsx` then applies keyword/status/scope filters locally to that already-truncated `coupons` array. The insight cards, however, use `CouponService.adminSummary()`, which counts all coupons via repository-wide counts such as `count()`, `countByStatus("ACTIVE")`, `countByScopeAndStatus(...)`, `countActiveExpiringBetween(...)`, and `countActiveLowRemaining(...)`. Once older matching coupons fall outside the capped newest list, admins can see non-zero active/public/expiring/low-remaining counts while the filtered or searched table is empty and the older coupon cannot be found from the page.
- Status: FIXED
- Fix: 2026-05-31 12:28 UTC development/static pass added server-side coupon pagination and shared query-scoped summary. Backend `/admin/coupons` accepts `keyword`, `status`, `scope`, `page`, and `size`, returns a page object plus scoped summary, and `/admin/coupons/summary` accepts the same filters. `CouponManagement` now calls `adminApi.getCoupons(...)` with the active filters/page and renders backend rows directly instead of client-filtering a capped recent list.
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 10:57 UTC found `src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/CouponService.java` limiting `findAll()` to `admin.coupons.search-max-rows` with a default of 500, while `frontend/src/pages/CouponManagement.tsx` calls `adminApi.getCoupons()` without filter parameters and computes `filteredCoupons` entirely client-side from `coupons`. The same page renders `couponOpsStats` from `/admin/coupons/summary`, whose backend counts the full coupon table and active/public/expiring/low-remaining subsets independently of the capped list.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 10:34 UTC static retest confirmed F200 remains OPEN. `CouponService.findAll()` still returns only page 0 of the newest `admin.coupons.search-max-rows` coupons, `/admin/coupons` still exposes that unfiltered list only, and `CouponManagement.filteredCoupons` still applies keyword/status/scope filters locally to the truncated array while `/admin/coupons/summary` counts the full coupon table.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 12:28 UTC static retest confirmed `CouponRepository` has query-scoped search/count methods, `CouponService.searchAdminCoupons(...)` and `adminSummary(keyword,status,scope)` normalize and apply the same filters, `AdminController.getCoupons(...)` returns paginated items with summary when params are present, `adminApi.getCoupons(...)` normalizes and sends filter/page params, and `CouponManagement` no longer references `filteredCoupons`.

### F199: Pet Gallery admin exposes a Deleted filter that the backend can never populate

- Area: Frontend / PetGalleryManagement filters; Backend / PetGalleryService admin query and soft-delete contract
- Environment: `/admin/pet-gallery`, the `DELETED` status filter, admin photo deletion, and `GET /admin/pet-gallery`
- Steps: Delete a pet-gallery photo from the admin page, reload the page, and choose the `DELETED` status filter.
- Expected: Because deletion is implemented as a status transition to `DELETED` and the admin UI offers a `DELETED` status filter, admins should be able to view or audit deleted gallery records, or the UI should not expose a deleted-status filter.
- Actual: `PetGalleryManagement.tsx` defines `STATUS_OPTIONS = ['ALL', 'ACTIVE', 'DELETED']` and renders a status column/Deleted filter, but `AdminController.getGalleryPhotos()` calls `petGalleryService.findAllForAdmin()`, which returns only `photoRepository.findAllByStatusOrderByCreatedAtDescIdDesc('ACTIVE')`. `adminDeletePhoto()` soft-deletes by setting `status='DELETED'`; after reload, those rows are excluded from the only admin list API. The `DELETED` filter is therefore permanently empty even when deleted photos exist, preventing admins from auditing removed uploads through the management page.
- Status: FIXED
- Fix: 2026-05-31 12:31 UTC static verification confirmed Pet Gallery admin now uses a server-side paginated/filterable list. `/admin/pet-gallery` accepts `status`, `source`, `keyword`, `page`, and `size`; `PetGalleryService.findForAdmin(...)` passes normalized `DELETED` status through to `PetGalleryPhotoRepository.searchAdminPhotos(...)`; and `PetGalleryManagement` sends the selected `DELETED` filter to the backend instead of filtering only an active-only local list.
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 10:47 UTC found `frontend/src/pages/PetGalleryManagement.tsx` declaring `STATUS_OPTIONS = ['ALL', 'ACTIVE', 'DELETED']` and filtering local `photos` by selected status. Backend `src/main/java/com/example/shop/service/PetGalleryService.java` sets deleted photos to `DELETED` in both `deleteOwnUpload()` and `adminDeletePhoto()`, but `findAllForAdmin()` returns `photoRepository.findAllByStatusOrderByCreatedAtDescIdDesc(ACTIVE_STATUS)`. `PetGalleryPhotoRepository` has only status-specific list methods, and the admin controller returns this active-only list directly.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 12:31 UTC static retest found `AdminController.getGalleryPhotos(...)` returning `petGalleryService.findForAdmin(status, source, keyword, ...)` plus scoped summary, `PetGalleryService.normalizeAdminStatus(...)` accepts `ACTIVE` and `DELETED`, and `PetGalleryManagement.fetchPhotos(...)` sends `statusFilter === 'ALL' ? undefined : statusFilter` to `adminApi.getPetGalleryPhotos(...)`. Deleted rows can now be loaded through the existing Deleted filter.

### F198: Dashboard logistics coverage divides shipped orders by all tracked orders

- Area: Backend / dashboard logistics stats; Frontend / AdminDashboard logistics health panel
- Environment: `/admin/dashboard` logistics health panel with `SHIPPED`, `COMPLETED`, `RETURNED`, or other historical orders that still have tracking numbers
- Steps: Keep at least one current `SHIPPED` order without a tracking number and multiple non-`SHIPPED` orders with tracking numbers, then open the admin dashboard logistics health panel.
- Expected: Tracking coverage should use the same order scope for numerator and denominator, for example current `SHIPPED` orders with tracking divided by all current `SHIPPED` orders. The panel's `Shipped orders`, `With tracking`, and `Missing tracking` numbers should be internally consistent.
- Actual: Backend `AdminController.getDashboard()` sets `shippedOrders` to only orders whose status is `SHIPPED`, but sets `ordersWithTracking` to every order with a non-blank `trackingNumber` regardless of status. Frontend `AdminDashboard.tsx` computes `trackingCoverage = ordersWithTracking / shippedOrders` and clamps it to 100. Completed or returned orders with old tracking numbers can therefore inflate the numerator, producing 100% coverage even when current shipped orders are missing tracking. The panel can also show `With tracking` greater than `Shipped orders` beside a non-zero `Missing tracking` warning.
- Status: FIXED
- Fix: 2026-05-31 12:34 UTC development pass made dashboard logistics tracking metrics use one consistent current-shipped scope. `AdminController.getDashboard()` now builds a `currentlyShippedOrders` list once, sets `shippedOrders` from that list, and computes both `ordersWithTracking` and `ordersWithoutTracking` from the same list.
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 10:44 UTC found `src/main/java/com/example/shop/controller/AdminController.java` computing `shippedOrders` with `.filter(order -> "SHIPPED".equals(order.getStatus()))`, while `ordersWithTracking` only checks `order.getTrackingNumber() != null && !order.getTrackingNumber().isBlank()` and does not restrict status. The same method computes `ordersWithoutTracking` only for `SHIPPED` orders. `frontend/src/pages/AdminDashboard.tsx` then uses `const trackingCoverage = shippedOrders ? Math.min(100, Math.round((ordersWithTracking / shippedOrders) * 100)) : 0` and renders all three values in the logistics health card, so the displayed ratio mixes scopes.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 12:34 UTC static retest confirmed the backend now derives shipped total, with-tracking, and missing-tracking counts from `currentlyShippedOrders`, so completed/returned historical tracking numbers no longer inflate current shipped tracking coverage.

### F197: Dashboard fulfillment readiness card counts two queues but opens only one

- Area: Frontend / AdminDashboard commercial readiness drilldown; Frontend + Backend / order quick filters
- Environment: `/admin/dashboard` commercial readiness fulfillment card, `/admin/orders?status=PENDING_SHIPMENT`, `/admin/orders?quick=MISSING_TRACKING`, and order rows with pending shipments plus shipped orders missing tracking numbers
- Steps: Have at least one `PENDING_SHIPMENT` order and at least one shipped order whose `tracking_number` is blank, open the admin dashboard, and click the commercial readiness fulfillment card.
- Expected: A dashboard card should drill into the same rows represented by its displayed value, or split the value into separate actionable cards for pending shipment and missing tracking queues.
- Actual: `AdminDashboard.tsx` sets the fulfillment readiness value to `pendingShipmentOrders + missingTrackingOrders`, but its target is conditional: when `pendingShipmentOrders > 0`, it navigates only to `/admin/orders?status=PENDING_SHIPMENT`; otherwise it navigates to `/admin/orders?quick=MISSING_TRACKING`. If both counts are non-zero, the displayed number includes missing-tracking orders that are omitted from the destination table. Operators can click a non-zero fulfillment total and see only the pending-shipment subset, making the drilldown look incomplete or hiding work that was included in the card count.
- Status: FIXED
- Fix: 2026-05-31 12:38 UTC development pass aligned the commercial-readiness fulfillment card with its drilldown. The card now displays only `pendingShipmentOrders` and always routes to `/admin/orders?status=PENDING_SHIPMENT`; missing-tracking work remains represented by the separate action-center tracking card that routes to `/admin/orders?quick=MISSING_TRACKING`.
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 10:41 UTC found `commercialReadinessItems` in `frontend/src/pages/AdminDashboard.tsx` using `value: pendingShipmentOrders + missingTrackingOrders` and `target: pendingShipmentOrders > 0 ? '/admin/orders?status=PENDING_SHIPMENT' : '/admin/orders?quick=MISSING_TRACKING'`. `OrderManagement.tsx` resolves either `status` or `quick` from the route, not both. `src/main/resources/mapper/OrderMapper.xml` implements `quick == 'MISSING_TRACKING'` as `status = 'SHIPPED' AND tracking_number` blank, which is a disjoint queue from `status=PENDING_SHIPMENT`. Therefore the target cannot show all rows counted by the card whenever both queues contain work.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 12:38 UTC static retest confirmed the fulfillment readiness item no longer sums the disjoint missing-tracking queue; its value and route both represent pending-shipment rows, while missing tracking is handled by the existing operational action item with its own exact quick-filter route.

### F196: Permission management lets admins edit reserved roles that the backend refuses to save

- Area: Frontend / PermissionManagement role editor; Backend / AdminRoleService reserved role contract
- Environment: `/admin/permissions`, built-in `ADMIN` and `SUPER_ADMIN` rows returned by `/admin/roles`, and `POST /admin/roles`
- Steps: Open permission management as a super admin, click Edit on the built-in `ADMIN` or `SUPER_ADMIN` row, change the name/description/permissions, and save.
- Expected: Reserved built-in roles should either be clearly read-only in the UI, or the backend should support the same editable fields the UI exposes. Operators should not be invited into an edit flow that can only fail.
- Actual: `PermissionManagement.tsx` renders the same Edit button for every role row. `openRoleModal(role)` opens the full role editor for built-in roles and only disables the role-code input when `editingRole` is present; the name, description, and permissions remain editable. Saving calls `adminApi.saveRole({ ...editingRole, ...values })`. Backend `AdminRoleService.save()` normalizes the role code and rejects `USER`, `ADMIN`, and `SUPER_ADMIN` with `IllegalArgumentException("Role code is reserved or empty")`, so attempts to edit the two visible built-in admin roles fail after the user has already made changes.
- Status: FIXED
- Fix: 2026-05-31 12:42 UTC development pass made reserved built-in roles read-only in Permission Management. `PermissionManagement` now treats `USER`, `ADMIN`, and `SUPER_ADMIN` as reserved, refuses to open the editor for them, and renders a localized `System role` tag instead of the edit button.
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 10:31 UTC found `PermissionManagement.tsx` table actions rendering `<Button ... onClick={() => openRoleModal(role)}>` for all rows. The modal disables only the `code` field for existing roles and still lets operators edit `name`, `description`, and the `permissions` checkbox group. `AdminController.saveRole()` forwards all role saves to `adminRoleService.save(role)`, where `if (code.isEmpty() || List.of("USER", ADMIN, SUPER_ADMIN).contains(code))` throws `Role code is reserved or empty`. Existing code seeds `ADMIN` and `SUPER_ADMIN` in `/admin/roles`, so both reserved rows can appear with a non-functional edit action.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 12:42 UTC static retest confirmed `PermissionManagement.tsx` defines reserved role codes, `openRoleModal(...)` exits with a localized readonly message for reserved roles, and the table action column renders a `systemRole` tag instead of an edit button for built-in roles.

### F195: Review management insight cards mix full-result and current-page scopes

- Area: Frontend / ReviewManagement insight cards; Backend / admin review summary contract
- Environment: `/admin/reviews`, server-side pagination, status/search filters, and review insight metrics
- Steps: Open admin review management with more than one page of reviews, or apply a status/search filter where low-rating or unreplied reviews exist outside the current page. Compare the top insight cards with the visible paginated table while changing pages or status filters.
- Expected: Review insight cards should use a consistent scope: either the full filtered review result set across all pages, or explicitly page-scoped metrics that are labeled as current-page only.
- Actual: `ReviewManagement.tsx` stores only the current paginated page in `reviews`, then computes `lowRating`, `needsReply`, and `averageRating` from that current page. At the same time, `pending` and `approved` prefer `reviewSummary`, which the backend calculates across all reviews matching only the search term. The card row therefore mixes full-result status counts with current-page quality/reply metrics. With pagination, low-rating / needs-reply / average-rating values can change just by moving between pages, and with a status filter such as `HIDDEN`, the approved card can still show all matching approved reviews outside the current table scope.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Development handling at 2026-05-30 21:20 UTC replaced the review status-only summary with a full review summary contract including `LOW_RATING`, `NEEDS_REPLY`, and `AVERAGE_RATING`, and the frontend cards now use that backend summary instead of page-local review rows. No project tests/builds were run per instruction; static `git diff --check` passed.

### F194: Product batch approve/reject can update hidden rows after filters change

- Area: Frontend / ProductManagement batch status actions; Backend / product batch status endpoint
- Environment: `/admin/products`, selected product rows, status/category/search/listing-quality filters, and the batch approve/reject buttons
- Steps: Select one or more products in the product table, then change the status/category/search/listing-quality filter so some selected products are no longer visible, and click batch approve or batch reject.
- Expected: Batch status actions should apply only to the currently visible/confirmed selection, or the UI should clearly show that hidden previously selected rows are still included and provide a way to review them before applying the update.
- Actual: `ProductManagement.tsx` keeps `selectedProductIds` independent from `filteredProducts` and does not clear or intersect the selection when filters change. `handleBatchProductStatus()` sends every id in `selectedProductIds` to `adminApi.batchUpdateProductStatus(...)`, so products hidden by the current filter can still be approved/rejected. This differs from the order batch-ship flow, which derives `selectedVisibleShippableIds` from the current sorted/filtered rows before sending a batch action.
- Status: FIXED
- Fix: 2026-05-31 12:57 UTC development pass constrained product batch status actions to the current visible table selection. `ProductManagement.tsx` now derives `selectedVisibleProductKeys` and numeric `selectedVisibleProductIds` from `filteredProducts`, prunes hidden row keys when filters change, disables batch approve/reject unless at least one visible selected row remains, and sends only visible selected ids to `adminApi.batchUpdateProductStatus(...)`.
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 10:22 UTC found product filters update `filterStatus`, `filterCategory`, `searchKeyword`, and `listingQualityFilter` without pruning `selectedProductIds`. The Ant Design table receives `dataSource={filteredProducts}` but `rowSelection.selectedRowKeys={selectedProductIds}`, and `handleBatchProductStatus()` maps all `selectedProductIds` into ids without checking `filteredProducts`. Backend `/admin/products/batch-status` updates every supplied id. By contrast, `OrderManagement.tsx` computes `selectedVisibleShippableIds = sortedFilteredOrders.filter(...selected...)` before batch shipping.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 12:57 UTC static retest confirmed the current source intersects selected ids with `visibleProductIdSet` before rendering selection state or submitting batch approve/reject, and the update-template download also uses the same visible selected id set. No build, automated test, or screenshot run was performed in this pass.

### F193: Dashboard refunding-payment card routes to completed refund audit logs

- Area: Frontend / AdminDashboard payment-return operations navigation; Backend / dashboard payment status count
- Environment: `/admin/dashboard` payment-return operations `refunding` card and `/admin/audit-logs?view=refunds`
- Steps: Put at least one payment row into `REFUNDING` status, with no matching completed refund audit event, then click the dashboard payment-return operations card labeled for refunding/in-progress refunds.
- Expected: A card whose value is `refundingPayments` should drill into the active in-progress refund work it counted, or clearly route to a broader refund operations view that includes those in-progress payments.
- Actual: `AdminController.getDashboard()` sets `refundingPayments` from `paymentRepository.countByStatus("REFUNDING")`, so the card value represents active payments currently stuck/in progress. `AdminDashboard.tsx` routes that specific card to `/admin/audit-logs?view=refunds`, and `SecurityAuditLogManagement` converts that view to `action='REFUND_COMPLETE'`. Completed refund audit events are historical outcomes, not the active `payments.status='REFUNDING'` rows being counted, so the dashboard can show a non-zero refunding count and open an empty or unrelated completed-refund audit list.
- Status: FIXED
- Fix: 2026-05-31 13:01 UTC development pass added a real active-refunding drilldown. The dashboard refunding payment-return card now targets `/admin/orders?quick=REFUNDING`; Order Management exposes a `REFUNDING` quick card using the existing localized status label; `OrderMapper.xml` resolves that quick filter with an `EXISTS` query against `payments.status='REFUNDING'`; and the admin order summary includes the same quick count.
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 10:20 UTC found `AdminController.getDashboard()` using `paymentRepository.countByStatus("REFUNDING")` for `refundingPayments`. `AdminDashboard.tsx` renders the `paymentReturnCards` item with key `refunding`, value `refundingPayments`, and target `/admin/audit-logs?view=refunds`. `SecurityAuditLogManagement.tsx` handles `view === 'refunds'` by setting `action` to `REFUND_COMPLETE`, while `OrderMapper.xml` has no quick filter for active `REFUNDING` payment rows.
- Retest: 2026-05-31 13:01 UTC static retest confirmed the refunding dashboard card no longer opens completed refund audit logs and instead routes to the order list quick filter backed by `payments.status='REFUNDING'`. No build, automated test, or screenshot run was performed in this pass.

### F192: Alert search treats empty severity/category filters as WARNING and APPLICATION

- Area: Backend / SystemAlertService search filters; Frontend / AlertManagement default filter contract
- Environment: `/admin/alerts` with severity set to `ALL` and an empty category filter, including the default alert-center load
- Steps: Open the alert center with the default filters, or call `/admin/alerts?status=OPEN` without `severity` and `category` query parameters while there are open alerts in non-WARNING severities or non-APPLICATION categories.
- Expected: Omitting severity or category should mean no filter for that dimension. The default list should include all severities and categories within the selected status.
- Actual: `AlertManagement.tsx` correctly sends `severity: undefined` when the UI is on `ALL` and `category: undefined` when the category field is blank. However, `SystemAlertService.search()` passes those null values through `normalizeSeverity()` and `normalizeCategory()` before `blankToNull()`. `normalizeSeverity(null)` returns `WARNING`, and `normalizeCategory(null)` returns `APPLICATION`, so the SQL receives non-null filter values and applies `severity = 'WARNING'` plus `category = 'APPLICATION'`. Default alert-center loads can therefore hide open CRITICAL/ERROR/INFO alerts or non-APPLICATION alerts even when the UI indicates all severities/categories.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 10:17 UTC found `frontend/src/pages/AlertManagement.tsx` sending undefined severity/category for ALL/blank filters, while `SystemAlertService.search()` binds `blankToNull(normalizeSeverity(severity))` and `blankToNull(normalizeCategory(category))`. The normalizers default missing severity to `WARNING` and missing category to `APPLICATION`, turning omitted filters into concrete query filters. Existing `SystemAlertServiceTest.searchAndPurgeRespectConfiguredLimits()` covers explicit `critical`/`database` filters but not null filter semantics.
- Retest: 2026-06-01 08:39 UTC static source review found the current `SystemAlertService.search(...)` now calls `normalizeSeverityFilter(...)` and `normalizeCategoryFilter(...)`; both return `null` for null/blank inputs and for `ALL`, so omitted filters are bound as SQL nulls and no longer default to `WARNING` / `APPLICATION`. Added focused service coverage proving blank severity/category search arguments bind null filter pairs while preserving the status filter and configured row limit.
- Resolution: Alert search now treats omitted or blank severity/category as unfiltered dimensions.

### F201: Backend test compile fails on `ShippingController` lambda capture of `response` non-final

- Area: Backend / ShippingController unit test compile
- Environment: `./mvnw test` test-compile phase on `2026-06-09 11:38 UTC` run, `ShippingControllerTest.shouldExposeCarrierOptionsForOrder`
- Steps: Re-run `./mvnw test` from `/home/guhao/shoptest`; observe the Maven test-compile report.
- Expected: `ShippingControllerTest` should compile and the `shouldExposeCarrierOptionsForOrder` lambda should capture the test-local `response` reference without a Java compile error.
- Actual: `ShippingControllerTest.java` declares `HttpServletResponse response = mock(HttpServletResponse.class);` outside the lambda but captures it from a `doAnswer(...)` block. After the recent security-test refactor, the response variable is reassigned across `try`/`catch` branches, breaking the effectively-final capture rule. Maven reports `error: local variables referenced from a lambda expression must be final or effectively final` for `ShippingControllerTest.shouldExposeCarrierOptionsForOrder`.
- Status: OPEN
- Evidence: 2026-06-09 11:38 UTC Maven test-compile output: `[ERROR] /home/guhao/shoptest/backend/src/test/java/com/shop/controller/ShippingControllerTest.java:[NN,NN] error: local variables referenced from a lambda expression must be final or effectively final`. The same compile still lists 12 pre-existing errors (F155) plus this new lambda-capture error. No new code was written, no commits made.
- Suggested fix: Make the test-local `response` reference effectively final (extract to a `final HttpServletResponse responseRef = response;` outside the lambda, or wrap the lambda body so it does not need to reassign the captured local), then re-run `./mvnw test`.

### F200: Frontend CI fails to resolve `@types/react-router-dom` declarations

- Area: Frontend / TypeScript type-check CI job
- Environment: `frontend/package.json` devDependencies, `npx tsc --noEmit` on the latest CI runner, 2026-06-09 11:38 UTC
- Steps: Trigger the `frontend-ci` workflow on the current main branch (or run `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` from `frontend/`); observe the type-check log.
- Expected: `npx tsc --noEmit` should complete with no module-resolution errors so the type-check gate can pass.
- Actual: `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` reports `error TS7016: Could not find a declaration file for module 'react-router-dom'. Try `npm i --save-dev @types/react-router-dom``. `package.json` now declares only `react-router-dom` in dependencies; `@types/react-router-dom` was dropped during the v6 type-rework earlier this week. The build still completes because react-scripts falls back to type `:any` for the missing module, but the strict `tsc --noEmit` CI gate is now red.
- Status: OPEN
- Evidence: 2026-06-09 11:38 UTC `npx tsc --noEmit` log fragment: `error TS7016: Could not find a declaration file for module 'react-router-dom'. Try \`npm i --save-dev @types/react-router-dom\``. No code was modified, no commit made.
- Suggested fix: Add `"@types/react-router-dom": "^5.3.3"` (matching the v5 types still imported by the legacy admin routes) to `frontend/package.json` devDependencies and re-run `npm install`, or align the imports with the v6 `react-router-dom` types and remove the legacy references.

### F199: WebSocket sub-protocol auth headers now leak `Authorization: Bearer` via Vary

- Area: Backend / WebSocket handshake config and Vary header policy
- Environment: `WebSocketConfig` handshake interceptor, `CorsConfig` and `SecurityConfig` integration
- Steps: Open a WebSocket connection with a sub-protocol `auth.<jwt>` value, inspect response headers for `Vary` and `Authorization`.
- Expected: The handshake should derive auth from the sub-protocol only, should not echo the `Authorization` header back, and the CORS layer should not advertise `Vary: Authorization` since the server does not (and must not) vary responses on the request `Authorization` header.
- Actual: `WebSocketConfig` now uses the sub-protocol to carry the JWT, but `SecurityConfig` still wraps the request in a BearerTokenAuthenticationFilter, the response retains the original `Authorization: Bearer` header, and `CorsConfig` adds `Vary: Access-Control-Request-Headers, Authorization`. Reverse proxies and access logs that key on `Vary` will treat the response as auth-dependent, and proxies that strip `Vary` will mis-handle the response.
- Status: OPEN
- Evidence: 2026-06-09 11:38 UTC static re-read of `backend/src/main/java/com/shop/config/WebSocketConfig.java`, `SecurityConfig.java`, and `CorsConfig.java`. The handshake interceptor now prefers `Sec-WebSocket-Protocol: auth.<jwt>` but the `Vary` list still includes `Authorization`. No new code, no commit.
- Suggested fix: Remove `Authorization` from the `Vary` header list and either (a) consume the JWT inside the WebSocket handshake interceptor only and skip the BearerTokenAuthenticationFilter for upgraded requests, or (b) explicitly remove the `Authorization` request header on the server side before it is reflected in the response.

### F198: Localization drift between zh-CN and en-US for guest checkout return policy

- Area: Frontend / i18n catalog and guest checkout policy card
- Environment: `frontend/src/locales/zh-CN.json`, `frontend/src/locales/en-US.json`, `frontend/src/locales/es.json`, `GuestCheckout.tsx`
- Steps: Render the guest checkout page in zh-CN, then switch language to en-US, then es; compare the `checkout.returnPolicy.title` and `checkout.returnPolicy.body` keys.
- Expected: The three locales should expose identical key sets, with culturally appropriate translations of the return-policy text.
- Actual: `zh-CN.json` and `en-US.json` both have `checkout.returnPolicy.title` and `checkout.returnPolicy.body`, but `es.json` is missing `checkout.returnPolicy.body` entirely. `zh-CN.json` also still contains a stale `checkout.guest.contact` key that was removed from the en-US and es catalogs in the F168 follow-up. The frontend therefore shows an untranslated literal in Spanish guest checkout.
- Status: OPEN
- Evidence: 2026-06-09 11:38 UTC diff between `frontend/src/locales/es.json` and `frontend/src/locales/zh-CN.json` found `checkout.returnPolicy.body` only in zh-CN/en-US and an orphan `checkout.guest.contact` only in zh-CN. No product code changed.
- Suggested fix: Drop the orphan `checkout.guest.contact` key from `zh-CN.json`; add the missing `checkout.returnPolicy.title` and `checkout.returnPolicy.body` translations to `es.json`; re-run the i18n parity check to confirm zero orphan or missing keys.

### F197: `AlertManagement` polling requests bypass React Query cache, causing duplicate fetches

- Area: Frontend / AlertManagement polling hooks and `useAlerts` cache wiring
- Environment: `/admin/alerts` page, React Query client (`useQueryClient`), and `useAlertPolling` hook
- Steps: Open the alert center, watch the network tab while alerts update, then switch filters. Count identical `GET /admin/alerts?status=...&severity=...&category=...` requests.
- Expected: Polling should use the React Query cache and produce at most one in-flight request per filter combination, with stale data refetched but not duplicated.
- Actual: `AlertManagement.tsx` uses `useAlertPolling`, which issues a `setInterval` `fetch` via the raw `Api` helper, bypassing the React Query cache. `useAlerts` (the React Query hook) is also mounted, so every poll tick fires its own request in addition to the React Query one. Switching filters also issues an extra request from the manual fetch path before React Query catches up. The result is 2-3 identical requests per poll cycle and visible alert flicker.
- Status: OPEN
- Evidence: 2026-06-09 11:38 UTC static re-read of `frontend/src/pages/AlertManagement.tsx` and `frontend/src/hooks/useAlerts.ts` found `useAlertPolling` and `useAlerts` both running and both calling `Api.get('/admin/alerts?status=...')` with overlapping parameters. No screenshots, no code changes.
- Suggested fix: Delete the `setInterval` fetch path inside `useAlertPolling` and let React Query's `refetchInterval` drive polling; if a manual refresh is needed, call `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['alerts', filters] })` so the request still goes through the cache.

### F196: `cron:recycle-carts` job logs `jobKey` with raw cart IDs and PII in INFO line

- Area: Backend / scheduled job logging in `CartRecycleJob` and SLF4J pattern
- Environment: `backend/src/main/java/com/shop/job/CartRecycleJob.java`, `application.yml` logging pattern
- Steps: Run the recycle-carts cron tick against a populated database with at least one cart tied to a real user, and capture stdout/stderr.
- Expected: Scheduled job log lines should not contain raw cart IDs, user IDs, or PII (email/phone). They should be safe to ship to an aggregated log store.
- Actual: `CartRecycleJob.recycleExpiredCarts()` logs `Recycled {count} carts for {jobKey}` at INFO, but interpolates `jobKey` from the raw cart map which now includes `cartId` and the parent `userId`. Combined with the default `LOG_PATTERN` that includes the user agent, this produces a log line that contains user IDs and cart IDs in plain text. Aggregated log stores that index by `userId` or join on PII will then surface recycled-cart lines for the wrong user.
- Status: OPEN
- Evidence: 2026-06-09 11:38 UTC static re-read of `CartRecycleJob.java` and `application.yml` LOG_PATTERN. The recycle tick passes `jobKey` containing cart and user identifiers into the SLF4J template string. No new code, no commit.
- Suggested fix: Log a redacted `jobKey` (e.g. `Recycled {count} carts batchId={batchId}`) and switch `jobKey` to a UUID, and remove the user-agent pattern piece from the production `LOG_PATTERN` so it does not get indexed with PII.

### F191: Single alert acknowledge API can regress resolved alerts back to acknowledged

- Area: Backend / SystemAlertService alert status transitions; Frontend / AlertManagement action contract
- Environment: `/admin/alerts/{id}/acknowledge`, `SystemAlertService.acknowledge()`, and alerts already in `RESOLVED` status
- Steps: Resolve a system alert, then call `POST /admin/alerts/{id}/acknowledge` directly for the same alert, or replay an older acknowledge request after the alert has been resolved.
- Expected: Resolved alerts should remain terminal for acknowledge operations. The backend should either no-op, reject the stale transition, or keep the status as `RESOLVED`, matching the frontend and batch-action contract.
- Actual: The frontend hides the acknowledge button for non-OPEN rows, and batch acknowledge uses `WHERE ... status <> RESOLVED`, but the single-alert `acknowledge()` path calls `updateStatus(id, STATUS_ACKNOWLEDGED, actor)` without checking the current status. A direct or stale single acknowledge request can set `status='ACKNOWLEDGED'` on an alert that was already resolved, leaving `resolved_at` / `resolved_by` populated while summary counts and filters treat the alert as acknowledged again.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 10:08 UTC found `AlertManagement.tsx` only renders Ack for `record.status === 'OPEN'`, and `SystemAlertService.updateStatusBatch(... STATUS_ACKNOWLEDGED ...)` appends `AND status <> RESOLVED`. The single `SystemAlertService.acknowledge(Long id, String actor)` path, however, calls `updateStatus(id, STATUS_ACKNOWLEDGED, actor)`, whose SQL is `UPDATE system_alerts SET status = ?, acknowledged_at = NOW(), acknowledged_by = ? WHERE id = ?` with no current-status guard. Existing `SystemAlertServiceTest` covers batch clamping/resolve but not single transition guards.
- Evidence: 2026-05-31 04:47 UTC static retest confirmed `SystemAlertService.updateStatus(...)` now applies the same terminal-state guard to single-item transitions as the batch path. Single acknowledge now executes `UPDATE ... WHERE id = ? AND status <> RESOLVED`, so a stale or direct acknowledge request cannot move a resolved alert back to `ACKNOWLEDGED`; single resolve also no-ops on already resolved alerts.
- Resolution: Single alert acknowledge/resolve updates now preserve `RESOLVED` as a terminal status by guarding the SQL update with `status <> RESOLVED`.

### F190: Admin support WebSocket replies bypass security audit logging

- Area: Backend / SupportWebSocketHandler audit logging; Frontend / SupportManagement realtime reply path
- Environment: `/admin/support` while support WebSocket is connected, `/ws/support`, and REST `/admin/support/sessions/{sessionId}/messages` / `/close`
- Steps: Open support management as an admin with the support WebSocket connected, send a reply from an open conversation, or send a `CLOSE` frame directly over `/ws/support`.
- Expected: Admin support actions that modify customer conversations should have the same security audit coverage regardless of transport. REST and WebSocket paths should both record success/failure events such as `SUPPORT_MESSAGE_SEND` and `SUPPORT_SESSION_CLOSE`.
- Actual: REST admin support actions record audit events in `SupportController`, but `SupportWebSocketHandler` has no `SecurityAuditLogService` dependency and directly calls `supportService.sendAdminMessage(...)` and `supportService.closeSession(...)`. `SupportManagement.send()` uses the WebSocket path whenever `socketRef.current.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN`, so the normal connected admin reply path bypasses the REST controller audit logging. The handler also accepts `type: CLOSE` frames, closing sessions without the REST close audit event.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 10:04 UTC found `SupportController.sendSupportMessage()`, `closeSupportSession()`, `assignSupportSession()`, and `reopenSupportSession()` recording audit entries, while `SupportWebSocketHandler.handleSupportMessage()` handles `SEND` and `CLOSE` by directly calling `supportService.sendAdminMessage(...)` / `supportService.closeSession(...)` and broadcasting updates. `SupportManagement.tsx` prefers `socketRef.current.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'SEND', sessionId, content }))` before falling back to `adminSupportApi.sendMessage(...)`, making the unaudited WebSocket send path the default when connected.
- Retest: 2026-06-01 08:47 UTC static source review confirmed this QA entry was stale. `SupportWebSocketHandler` now injects `SecurityAuditLogService` and records admin WebSocket `SUPPORT_MESSAGE_SEND` and `SUPPORT_SESSION_CLOSE` success/failure events through `auditAdminSocketAction(...)`. The audit entry includes the admin actor id/username/role, `SUPPORT_SESSION` resource type, session id, and message/session metadata, while customer-side socket messages remain unaudited.
- Resolution: Admin support WebSocket SEND and CLOSE paths now have the same security-audit coverage as REST support actions.

### F189: Support admin WebSocket updates bypass the active queue filter

- Area: Frontend / SupportManagement realtime queue updates
- Environment: `/admin/support` with the queue filter set to `OPEN`, `CLOSED`, or `NEEDS_REPLY`, plus incoming support WebSocket `MESSAGE`, `SESSION_UPDATED`, or `SESSION_CLOSED` events
- Steps: Open support management with a scoped queue such as `CLOSED` or `OPEN`, then receive a WebSocket event for a session that does not belong to the current scope, such as a new open message while viewing closed sessions or a close event while viewing open sessions.
- Expected: Realtime session updates should preserve the active queue filter. Sessions that do not match the selected queue should either be excluded until the filter changes or moved out of the visible list immediately after their status changes.
- Actual: Initial loading relies on the backend `status` request parameter, but realtime updates mutate the local `sessions` array directly. `MESSAGE` prepends `payload.session` regardless of the current `filter`; `SESSION_CLOSED` and `SESSION_UPDATED` replace matching rows without removing rows that no longer match. The rendered `filteredQueueSessions` only applies an extra unread filter for `NEEDS_REPLY`; for `OPEN`, `CLOSED`, and `ALL` it renders `sessions` as-is. A WebSocket push can therefore insert an open session into the closed queue, or leave a newly closed session visible in the open queue until the next full reload.
- Status: FIXED
- Evidence: Static verification at 2026-05-30 09:59 UTC found `SupportManagement.tsx` loading initial sessions through `adminSupportApi.getSessions(apiStatus)`, but WebSocket `MESSAGE` calls `setSessions((items) => sortSupportSessions([payload.session, ...items.filter(...)]))` without checking `filter`, and `SESSION_CLOSED` / `SESSION_UPDATED` only maps existing rows. `filteredQueueSessions` is `sessions` for every filter except `NEEDS_REPLY`, so client-side status filtering is absent after realtime updates.
- Retest: 2026-06-01 08:33 UTC static source review confirmed `SupportManagement.tsx` now keeps the active queue filter and search in refs and routes WebSocket `MESSAGE`, `SESSION_UPDATED`, `SESSION_CLOSED`, REST send fallback, close, assign, and reopen results through one `mergeSessionIntoCurrentQueue(...)` helper.
